Title: 'Eisob cholbe na': PM Modi says TMC has become carbon copy of Left in Murshidabad rally- Moneycontrol.com
Description: PM Modi said if one looks at Bengal’s political history, it showed that dominant forces eventually lose public support and asserted that the electorate would now turn against the TMC in a similar fashion.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday launched a scathing attack of the Trinamool Congress (TMC), accusing the ruling party of adopting the same governance practices as the Left and failing to deliver real transformation in West Bengal. Addressing an election rally in Murshidabad, PM Modi said if one looks at Bengal’s political history, it showed that dominant forces eventually lose public support and asserted that the electorate would now turn against the TMC in a similar fashion. "... Since independence, the arrogance of everyone who dared to challenge Bengal has been shattered. First, the British; then the Congress; and finally, the Leftists saw their arrogance crumble. Now, the people of Bengal will shatter the arrogance of the TMC...," said PM Modi. The Prime Minister further claimed that the TMC, which came to power on the slogan of “Ma Mati Manush,” had not lived up to expectations and instead replicated the functioning of the previous Left government. According to him, voters gave the party an opportunity with optimism, but once in office, it began operating in a manner indistinguishable from its predecessor. "The TMC became a carbon copy of the Left. All the goons and syndicates associated with the Left have migrated to the Trinamool... The TMC has now taken charge of everything—from the smuggling of weapons, narcotics, and cattle, to the cuts and commissions that were once the hallmark of the Left," he said. Reaffirming the BJP’s governance agenda, PM Modi said the party remained committed to introducing the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in West Bengal. He emphasized that national security was a top priority and asserted that implementing the UCC would end what he described as appeasement-driven politics in the state. Framing the BJP’s campaign as more than an electoral contest, Modi said the party’s push in Bengal aimed to dismantle what he termed the TMC’s “Jungle Raj” and “Syndicate Raj.” He described the BJP’s pledge as a broader political mission intended to bring structural change in governance. He added that the post-election scenario would usher in accountability, claiming that those protecting corruption would face consequences. Modi also said that women’s safety in the state would improve and warned that individuals shielding wrongdoing would not remain unchallenged after the results. The Prime Minister’s visit comes ahead of key polling phases in West Bengal scheduled for April 23 and April 29, with counting set for May 4. The contest follows the 2021 assembly elections, when the TMC secured a sweeping victory with 213 seats, while the BJP expanded its presence significantly by winning 77 seats — setting up a high-stakes battle in the current election cycle. Title: TCS stock outlook: Brokerages bullish post Q4 results, see steady growth, AI tailwinds; check target prices- Moneycontrol.com
Description: TCS Q4 Results Review: Analysts are bullish on AI-led opportunities and deal momentum, and cautious on sectoral weakness and structural pricing pressures.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Brokerages have largely maintained a constructive stance on Tata Consultancy Services’ stock following its Q4 FY26 results, citing stable margins, strong deal wins and rising AI contribution, although some concerns remain around growth visibility and margins. TCS reported a largely in-line Q4 FY26 performance, with revenue beating estimates, margins remaining stable and deal wins staying strong, reinforcing visibility on medium-term growth. CLSA retained an Outperform rating on the stock with a target price of Rs 2,985 per share, noting that the quarter was largely in line on revenue and EBIT margins. The brokerage highlighted that order bookings improved sequentially but declined year-on-year due to renewal seasonality. It pointed to GenAI revenue as a key medium- to long-term tailwind, now at an annualised $2.3 billion and contributing 7.5 percent of total revenue. CLSA also flagged TCS’ attractive free cash flow yield of around 6 percent and valuation discount to peers as supportive for its positive stance. Also read | TCS Q4 net profit jumps 29% QoQ to Rs 13,718 crore; revenue beats estimates; Rs 31 final dividend announced Goldman Sachs also maintained a Buy rating with a target price of Rs 2,710, noting no major surprises in Q4. It highlighted broad-based sequential growth and strong deal wins, including three mega deals, with early signs of stability in mid- and large-sized accounts. However, it flagged modest growth momentum at around 0.8 percent QoQ for the third straight quarter and limited margin expansion despite FX tailwinds. Further, the brokerage said that the AI reinvestment could keep margins under pressure. JPMorgan maintained an Overweight rating with a target price of Rs 3,150, calling the quarter strong with a revenue beat, stable margins and robust deal wins. The brokerage said management commentary indicates a gradual improvement into FY27, supported by stabilising demand across key sectors. It highlighted strong deal wins of $12 billion and improving international growth, while noting that AI revenue grew 28 percent sequentially to 7.5 percent of total revenue. JPMorgan also emphasised TCS’ capital return profile, with 80-100 percent of free cash flow returned via dividends and buybacks. Nomura reiterated a Buy rating with a target price of Rs 2,930, stating that Q4 performance was broadly in line, with strong deal wins driven by mega deals. The brokerage expects FY27 to be better than FY26, particularly for international markets, and sees margin stability despite continued reinvestment. It has raised FY27-28 EPS estimates by 2-3 percent and expects stronger growth in the first half of FY27. Nomura also increased its dollar revenue growth forecast to 3.8 percent for FY27 and 4.5 percent for FY28. However, HSBC maintained a more neutral stance with a Hold rating and a target price of Rs 2,755. It described the quarter as decent amid weak IT sector sentiment but acknowledged an improving demand outlook and exit rate that could support better performance in FY27. The brokerage expects mid- to low single-digit growth over the longer term. Jefferies remained cautious, retaining an Underperform rating with a target price of Rs 2,275. While it said revenue was in line, it flagged a margin miss versus estimates and highlighted weak BFSI growth and flat deal bookings as potential constraints on growth. The brokerage also raised concerns around AI-led revenue deflation and expects margins to remain rangebound, estimating an EPS CAGR of around 5.5 percent over FY26-29. While brokerages are aligned on the stability in TCS’ near-term performance and strong deal pipeline, they diverge on the sustainability of growth and margins. Analysts are bullish on AI-led opportunities and deal momentum, and cautious on sectoral weakness and structural pricing pressures. Title: TCS share price falls over 2% post Q4 results; brokerages see AI-led tailwinds, but flag growth, margin risks- Moneycontrol.com
Description: TCS stock price fall today comes amid mixed brokerage views on the sustainability of growth and margins, despite being supported by a strong deal pipeline and rising contribution from AI.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Shares of Tata Consultancy Services fell over 2.5 percent on Friday after the company reported its Q4 FY26 results, with brokerages maintaining a broadly constructive but cautious stance on the stock. TCS stock ended at Rs 2,523, down 2.55 percent for the day, even as its March quarter performance came largely in line with expectations, with a revenue beat, stable margins and strong deal wins. TCS reported an in-line Q4 FY26 performance, with revenue rising 5.4 percent QoQ to Rs 70,698 crore, beating estimates, while net profit jumped 29 percent QoQ to Rs 13,718 crore. Margins remained stable and deal wins were strong at $12 billion. The stock reaction comes amid mixed brokerage views on the sustainability of growth and margins, despite being supported by a strong deal pipeline and rising contribution from artificial intelligence-led services. Also read | TCS stock outlook: Brokerages bullish post Q4 results, see steady growth, AI tailwinds; check target prices Brokerages largely remain constructive, led by CLSA, JPMorgan, Nomura and Goldman Sachs, which retain positive ratings citing strong deal wins, improving demand outlook and AI as a key growth driver. They also highlighted stable margins and attractive valuations, though noting modest near-term growth with around 0.8 percent QoQ organic expansion. HSBC maintained a Hold rating, expecting steady but moderate growth, while Jefferies stayed cautious with an Underperform call, flagging weak BFSI trends, flat deal momentum and risks of margin pressure from AI-led reinvestment. While brokerages are aligned on long-term AI-led opportunities and deal visibility, they remain divided on the pace of growth recovery and margin trajectory. TCS stock has now declined around 22 percent over the past one year, significantly underperforming the Nifty 50, which has risen over 5.3 percent during the same period, reflecting persistent concerns around growth visibility in the IT sector. Title: Sarvam AI $250 million funding round: Bessemer Venture Partners joins while Accel opts out- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The Sarvam deal is being done by Bessemer’s US team. Their San Francisco-based Partner, Sameer Dholakia, is engaging with the company, which is being valued at $1.5 billion post-money, as first reported by Moneycontrol on March 24.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: US-based venture capital fund Bessemer Venture Partners has now expressed interest in joining Nvidia and HCLTech, along with a clutch of other investors, who are together investing $200-250 million into Indian foundation AI models upstart Sarvam, people aware of the developments told Moneycontrol. “The round is still being constructed but Bessemer has now shown interest to invest in Sarvam. The final close will still be a few weeks away, though,” one of the persons cited above said. The contours of the deal remains unchanged from earlier when Moneycontrol exclusively reported Sarvam is being valued at $1.5 billion post-money, up 7X in a span of two years. “The Sarvam deal is, however, being done by Bessemer’s United States (US) team. Their Partner from San Francisco, Sameer Dholakia, is engaging with the company,” the source added. Dholakia has been a Partner at Bessemer for four years now and is listed as an investor in AI giant Anthropic, Alphabet’s self-driving car unit Waymo, among several others. Moneycontrol was first to report Sarvam was in talks with Nvidia, HCLTech and Accel to raise $200-250 million at a 7x jump in valuation to $1.5 billion post-money on March 24. However, Accel has now dropped off but the other two strategic investors – Nvidia and HCLTech – are still engaging and progressing on the deal, sources said. Bessemer, Sarvam and Accel did not reply to Moneycontrol’s queries. If talks materialise, it would mark the first time in recent years that an Indian IT major has stepped in as a strategic investor in an AI startup -- potentially easing concerns about their pace on artificial intelligence (AI). Sarvam’s $200-250 million round is set to become the largest private market funding round for an Indian startup this year, while also marking the biggest capital infusion into a pure-play Indian AI startup to date. Sarvam’s round is set to become the largest private market funding round for an Indian startup this year, while also marking the biggest capital infusion into a pure-play Indian AI startup to date. This will be Sarvam’s first major cash infusion after about two-and-a-half years. It last raised $41 million in its Series A funding round, in December 2023, led by Lightspeed Ventures with participation from Peak XV Partners and Khosla Ventures. While the interest has been high since then, investor conviction around the Bengaluru-based startup has strengthened just weeks after it launched two large language models (LLMs), a 30-billion-parameter model and a 105-billion-parameter model, both trained from scratch in India, at the India AI Impact Summit in New Delhi in February. “(The models are) cheaper than something like a Gemini Flash, but outperforms it in many benchmarks,” Sarvam co-founder Pratyush Kumar had said earlier this year. “Even with something like Gemini 2.5 Flash, which is a bigger and more expensive model, we find that the Indian language performance of this model is even better.” Sarvam is a prominent artificial intelligence (AI) player from India. Conversational AI firm Gnani.ai, and IIT-Bombay-led consortium’s sovereign AI initiative BharatGen are the others building foundational AI models in India as domestic startups and national initiatives stepped up efforts to build homegrown alternatives to global AI systems dominated by Big Tech. Sarvam has also emerged as a key player in India’s sovereign artificial intelligence (AI) push. The IndiaAI Mission, backed by a Rs 10,000 crore (~$1 billion) fund to build India’s sovereign foundational model, has disbursed Rs 111 crore ($12 million) in GPU subsidies so far. The biggest winner to date is Bengaluru-based Sarvam AI , which bagged a record 4,096 NVIDIA H100 SXM GPUs via Yotta Data Services, receiving nearly Rs 99 crore ($11 million) in subsidies. Founded in July 2023 by Vivek Raghavan and Pratyush Kumar, who previously worked at Infosys co-founder Nandan Nilekani-backed AI4Bharat, Sarvam develops a full-stack offering for Generative AI, ranging from research-led innovations in training custom AI models to an enterprise-grade platform for authoring and deployment. Title: Saatvik Green Energy shares gain 6% on Rs 108.75 crore order win- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Saatvik Green Energy Share Price | The share price gained 26 percent over the past one month.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Saatvik Green Energy share price rose 6 percent in the early trade on April 10 following the company bagging order worth Rs 108.75 crore from Independent EPC players. At 09:21am, Saatvik Green Energy was quoting at Rs 447.75, up Rs 26.80, or 6.37 percent, on the BSE. Company's material subsidiary Saatvik Solar Industries has received and accepted an order aggregating to Rs 108.75 crore from one renowned independent power producers/EPC players for the supply of solar photovoltaic modules. The said order will be executed by September 2026. Catch all the market action on our live blog In the month of March 2026, the company has received four different orders aggregating to Rs 723.77 crore for the supply of Solar PV Modules. The stock closed at ₹420.95 in the previous trading session, gaining ₹2.40 or 0.57 percent. During the period, it touched a 52-week high of ₹580 on October 15, 2025, and a 52-week low of ₹329.70 on March 9, 2026. At the current price, the stock is trading 27.42 percent below its 52-week high, while it remains 27.68 percent above its 52-week low. The company’s market capitalisation stands at ₹5,350.49 crore. The stock has surged 26 percent over the past one month. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Railways' PSC sleeper maker Vishal Nirmiti receives SEBI nod for IPO- Moneycontrol.com
Description: SEBI issued its observations on April 9 on the draft papers filed by Vishal Nirmiti in September 2025.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Pune-based Vishal Nirmiti, a manufacturer of pre-stressed concrete (PSC) sleepers for railways, has received approval from the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) to proceed with its IPO plans. SEBI issued its observations on April 9 on the draft papers filed by the company in September 2025. The issuance of observations means the company can launch its initial public offering (IPO) for subscription within the next one year. Civil engineering, manufacturing, and construction company Vishal Nirmiti has proposed to raise Rs 125 crore by issuing fresh shares, while promoter entity Vaman Prestressing Company intends to sell up to 15 lakh shares via an offer-for-sale. Apart from manufacturing and dealing in pre-stressed concrete (PSC) sleepers for railways, the company is also involved in the production of precast and pre-stressed concrete products for various applications. It is also engaged in the fabrication and erection of mild steel pipes (MS pipes), MS liners, and penstock pipes for pumped storage projects (PSPs). Vishal Nirmiti had a consolidated order book of Rs 504.1 crore as of August 2025. The company plans to utilise the proceeds from the fresh issue for its working capital requirements, debt repayment, and general corporate purposes. Saffron Capital Advisors has been appointed as the book-running lead manager for managing the Vishal Nirmiti IPO. Meanwhile, the capital markets regulator is awaiting in-principle approval from stock exchanges on the draft papers of Jainam Broking, which had filed draft document with SEBI via the confidential route in October 2025. Further, it has sought comments from other regulators and government agencies on the draft documents of EAAA India Alternatives and Advanta Enterprises, which filed IPO papers in January 2026. Title: PM Modi pledges 7th Pay Commission rollout if BJP wins West Bengal: Here are his six guarantees- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The Prime Minister further said the upcoming West Bengal Assembly elections were aimed at restoring “Bengal’s glory” and building a “developed Bengal,” while accusing the TMC government of hampering industrial growth in the state.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, on Thursday, reiterated his attack on the Trinamool Congress (TMC)-led West Bengal government, describing it as a “nirmam sarkar,” while outlining what he called “six guarantees” for the state if the BJP comes to power. Addressing a public rally in Purba Medinipur, PM Modi said, “To usher in Viksit Bengal, it’s essential that TMC’s ‘nirmam sarkaar’ is removed from the state.” He said the first guarantee of a BJP government would be to end what he described as an atmosphere of fear. “A BJP government will replace this atmosphere of fear with trust. It will restore faith in the rule of law,” he said. The Prime Minister added that the second guarantee would ensure administrative accountability. “Under a BJP government, the administrative machinery will be accountable, fully answerable, to the public it serves,” he said. Listing his third assurance, PM Modi said all major corruption and crime-related cases would be reopened. “The files regarding every scam, every act of corruption, every injustice committed against our daughters, and every case of rape will be reopened,” he said. On the fourth guarantee, he said strict action would be taken against corruption regardless of position. “Whoever has engaged in corruption belongs in jail. Be it a minister or a watchman, the law will hold everyone to account. No TMC goon will be able to evade the law any longer,” he said. His fifth guarantee focused on citizenship and security issues. “Refugees will be granted every right and privilege guaranteed by the Constitution. However, infiltrators will be driven out; they will not be permitted to remain in India,” he said. As the sixth guarantee, PM Modi promised implementation of the 7th Pay Commission once a BJP government is formed in the state. “The moment a BJP government is formed here, we will implement the 7th Pay Commission,” he said. In a post on X, PM Modi also said, “Haldia’s energy reflects a strong wave for change across West Bengal. People are placing their trust in the BJP.” He also referred to political shifts in the state, including the BJP’s performance in Nandigram in 2021 and contests involving Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee in Bhabanipur, saying a “wave of change” was visible across West Bengal. The Prime Minister further said the upcoming West Bengal Assembly elections were aimed at restoring “Bengal’s glory” and building a “developed Bengal,” while accusing the TMC government of hampering industrial growth in the state. “This election in Bengal is not an ordinary election. It is an election to re-establish Bengal’s glory,” he said, alleging decline in industry and employment under the current government. The West Bengal Assembly elections will be held in two phases on April 23 and April 29, with counting scheduled for May 4. The state has 294 Assembly seats. In the previous elections in 2021, the Trinamool Congress won 213 seats, while the BJP secured 77 seats. The Congress and Left Front failed to win any seats. Title: OPINION | Khambhat, Gujarat’s lost port that was gateway to Strait of Hormuz- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Khambhat once thrived as a major port linking India to global trade networks before silting, shifting politics, and rising rivals led to its gradual decline
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: We are living at a moment when the ports on the far side of the Arabian Sea, especially those accessed via the Strait of Hormuz, are making international headlines. There is no better time than the present to explore the past of a Gujarat port which once received a huge volume of trade from the Persian Gulf countries and beyond. In a land known for its ports, from Kandla to Surat and more, there is one port that has faded from the list. Whenever the name of Khambhat comes up in history, it is as a source of immense wealth, and of men and their ambitions which rode the waves. Variously called Cambay, Cambaet, or Cambaia – depending on the accent of the arriving traveller – Khambhat was once among the most prosperous port towns on the western coast of India. Located at the northern end of the Gulf of Khambhat in present-day Gujarat, the town served for centuries as an important maritime gateway connecting India with the Persian Gulf, Arabia, East Africa, and parts of Southeast Asia and Europe. Not just Khambhat, but the entire region has been associated with maritime activity since antiquity. A mere 65 kilometres away is the Indus Valley port of Lothal, linked to overseas trade networks as early as the third millennium BCE. But from the 10th century CE to about the 16th–17th centuries, it was Khambhat that welcomed Persians, Arabs, Africans, and European merchants, travellers, and adventurers to its shores. Many of these travellers left behind accounts of what they saw in Khambhat. In the 10th century came Arab historian Al-Masʿudi, who described the city as a flourishing port. From him to Venetian Marco Polo (13th century), Moroccan Ibn Battuta (14th), Italian Niccolò de’ Conti (15th), Russian Afanasy Nikitin (also in the 15th century), to Portuguese Duarte Barbosa (16th century), the list is long and illustrious. And their descriptions of Khambhat are rich in detail. From the port, ships were loaded with cotton, silk, chintz, leather goods, agate beads, and indigo of the finest quality. Trade in the town was dominated by two groups – the Hindu Vanias and Jain traders on one side, and Muslim Bohra merchants on the other hand, with the former group being dominant. The Vanias had organised themselves into guilds, each representing a particular item, and were headed by a ‘Sheth’. Working with ingenuity, the Bohras and the Vanias were able to extend trade from the port almost in the shape of two giant, ever-moving tentacles, one of which went into the Malacca region and the other touching Aden. Painting of Dil Khush garden of Khambhat, drawn by James Forbes in 1770. (Source: Arjun Kumar) Khambhat’s prosperity also impacted its history. Thanks to the taxes paid by merchants and by pilgrims going to Mecca, Sultan Bahadur Shah of Gujarat had an overflowing treasury. His army also had matchlocks and artillery, with this technology and its experts also coming via the sea. In 1535, when the Mughal Empire was less than a decade old in India, Emperor Humayun was drawn by the wealth of Gujarat’s ports. As conflict loomed, Bahadur Shah was confident of his military strength in being able to face the Mughal force. While the Mughal military differentiator of artillery – which had helped them prevail over a numerically superior foe in the Battle of Panipat (1526) – was negated by the Gujarat army also having artillery, Mughal ranks were rapidly inflated by men seeking a share of Gujarat’s wealth. Eventually, the sheer weight of Mughal numbers prevailed. And then the Mughal force entered Khambhat. The wealth of Khambhat became its undoing, and it burnt for three days after the Mughal attack. It was then that the hitherto landlocked Mughals saw the sea for the first time and perhaps realised the opportunities it offered. Decades later, Humayun’s son Akbar also attacked Gujarat. His aim too was to attain a sea outlet for the empire and ensure that the state’s merchant wealth came to his empire. A British era educational building at Khambhat. (Source: Arjun Kumar) Contact with the sea became the channel for travellers from Syria, Turkey, and even European countries like Portugal to arrive at Mughal courts. Akbar specifically wanted to be on good terms with the Portuguese, who controlled sea traffic to Mecca through their domination of the Arabian Sea. Later, in 1612, the British adventurer William Hawkins would leave India from Khambhat, after four years of dodging Mughal court intrigue and Portuguese hostility while at Jahangir’s court. The British maintained a presence here, and in fact, the very first cricket match to be played in the Indian subcontinent is believed to have been at Khambhat, between British officers, in 1721. However, the prosperity that came with the sea also went away with it. From the 16th century onwards, Khambhat began to decline as a port. The principal cause of this was the silting of the harbour. Rivers flowing into the Gulf of Khambhat carried large amounts of sediment, which gradually filled the harbour and made navigation difficult for large ships. In fact, the land on which the town was built stands on the silt deposited by the Mahi River. After the town came up, the river continued to deposit silt, but this time in the port area, leading to silting over time. The Gulf of Khambhat is also known for strong tidal currents, which further complicated navigation. Politics too had a role. With the Mughals weakening and the British gaining the upper hand, the ports of Surat and Bombay were in the ascendant and easier to access, with deeper harbours allowing larger vessels to dock. What used to be the harbour at Khambhat - now entirely dry and used for salt farming. (Source: Arjun Kumar) Khambhat then began to be driven by the trade in agate beads, a business that continues there. In 1803, the place was ceded by the Marathas to the British and witnessed an increase in Gothic architecture. With the arrival of the railways in 1901, people began to look inland for opportunities. Old merchant families left the town, leaving behind grand buildings and memories of a lost prosperity. A drive through the town today reveals many stately houses, including fine Bohra mansions. One building that has stood the test of time and seen Khambhat’s ebb and flow is the Jama Masjid. Completed in 1325, when the place was still a bustling port with centuries of growth ahead of it, the mosque originally stood right next to the sea. Merchants would offer prayers at the mosque before embarking on voyages to Persia and Arabia. Pillared corridors inside Khambhat's Jama Masjid One of the few remaining examples of Tughlaq-period architecture in Gujarat, the mosque is built in an open courtyard style, its spacious design and the façade of the prayer hall with its three arches showing an intermingling of Islamic building traditions with local style. Evidence of temple material being used on the ceiling of Khambhat's Jama Masjid. (Source: Arjun Kumar) Today, merchants no longer look to the sea at Khambhat, which is 7 kilometres away from the town. The frenetic energy of a port has been replaced by a sleepy small-town flavour. Where ships once docked, we now find salt farms—and the memory of a lost port. (Arjun Kumar is a brand consultant by profession, he tweets @HiddenHeritage.) Title: OPINION | Congress–RSS relationship evolution reflected in Kharge’s ‘snake’ remark- Moneycontrol.com
Description: RSSFACT: Kharge criticised RSS and BJP sparking controversy while history shows shifting Congress-RSS ties from co-operation before independence to growing political rivalry in recent decades
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge recently compared the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) and its ideological mentee, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), to snakes. During an election campaign rally in Assam, he reportedly said, “The RSS and the BJP are venomous snakes... if a venomous snake is passing in front of you, even if you are offering prayer, you should pause the prayer and kill the snake.” The statement has drawn strong reactions from various quarters, with cases being filed against Kharge for making what are alleged to be defamatory and provocative remarks. Kharge is not the first Congress leader to target the RSS in recent times. Over the last couple of decades, as the Congress has taken what some describe as a more left-leaning stance, the RSS has been consistently criticised. It is worth examining how the relationship between the RSS and Congress has evolved over time. The RSS founder, Keshav Baliram Hedgewar, was a revolutionary who later joined Congress. He went to jail twice for participating in the freedom struggle. During the Quit India Movement, several Congress leaders were helped by the RSS as they went underground to evade arrest. Aruna Asaf Ali mentioned in an interview in the 1960s that she stayed at the residence of RSS Delhi unit in-charge Lala Hansraj Gupta during the movement to avoid arrest. During Partition, several Congress leaders reportedly sought the help of RSS volunteers to move safely from areas that were to become part of Pakistan, amid widespread communal violence. A. N. Bali of Lahore University, who migrated to India after Partition, provided a detailed first-hand account of these developments, noting that Congress leaders sought RSS assistance to protect their families. The RSS was banned shortly after India gained independence in 1948 on allegations of involvement in the assassination of Mahatma Gandhi. The ban was later lifted due to lack of evidence. During the Sino-Indian War, the RSS played a role in supporting national efforts. Jawaharlal Nehru, a critic of the RSS, invited the organisation to participate in the Republic Day Parade in 1963, which it did. In 1965, Lal Bahadur Shastri invited the second RSS Sarsanghchalak, M. S. Golwalkar, to an all-party meeting convened after Pakistan attacked India. Golwalkar was the only non-political individual invited. In 1966, the Congress government led by Prime Minister Indira Gandhi established a judicial commission to probe Mahatma Gandhi’s assassination. Headed by Justice J. L. Kapur, a retired Supreme Court judge, the commission examined 101 witnesses and 407 documents before publishing its report in 1969. Its key findings included: – “They (the accused) have not been proved to have been members of the RSS, nor has that organisation been shown to have had a hand in the murder.” (Vol. I, p. 186) – “...there is no evidence that the RSS as such was indulging in violent activities against Mahatma Gandhi or the top Congress leaders.” (Vol. I, p. 66) In 1971, the RSS passed a resolution supporting the Congress government led by Indira Gandhi in addressing the crisis in East Pakistan. During the Indo-Pak war that year, the RSS contributed to the national effort, as it had in earlier conflicts. In 1975, following the declaration of the The Emergency by Indira Gandhi, the RSS was banned again. The organisation participated in the underground movement opposing the Emergency. The Economist wrote on 12 December 1976: “The underground campaign against Mrs Gandhi claims to be the only non-left-wing revolutionary force in the world, disavowing both bloodshed and class struggle. The ground troops of this operation consist of tens of thousands of cadres organised to the village level into four-man cells. Most of them are RSS regulars, though more young recruits are joining. Other underground parties have largely abandoned the field to Jan Sangh and RSS.” In the 1980s, the RSS launched an anti-conversion movement under the banner of Hindu Virat Manch. It was headed by veteran Congress leader Karan Singh, while its general secretary was RSS pracharak Ashok Singhal, who later became widely known for leading the Ram Janmabhoomi movement. Veteran Congress leader Daudayal Khanna, a cabinet minister in the Uttar Pradesh government, also played a role in the launch of the Ram Janmabhoomi movement in 1983. In 1992, the RSS was banned for the third time by the Congress government following the demolition of a disputed structure in Ayodhya. The ban was later lifted. It is evident that over the decades, Congress has taken an increasingly adversarial stance towards the RSS. However, this shift is often interpreted by analysts as being influenced by electoral strategy as much as ideological conviction. The effectiveness of this approach continues to be debated, particularly in light of electoral outcomes in recent years. Earlier RSSFACTS columns can be read here . (Arun Anand has authored two books on the RSS. His X handle is @ArunAnandLive.) Views are personal, and do not represent the stand of this publication. Title: Nitish Kumar takes oath as Rajya Sabha member- Moneycontrol.com
Description: With this move, Kumar completes a rare political milestone, having served in all four legislative bodies in India: the Bihar Assembly, Bihar Legislative Council, Lok Sabha, and now the Rajya Sabha.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: In a significant political development, Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar on Friday took oath as a member of the Rajya Sabha, marking a major transition in the state’s political landscape. The oath was administered by Rajya Sabha Chairman CP Radhakrishnan in his chamber in the presence of senior leaders, including Union ministers JP Nadda, Nirmala Sitharaman, and Arjun Ram Meghwal. Leaders across parties, including Jairam Ramesh, were also present. With this move, Kumar completes a rare political milestone, having served in all four legislative bodies in India: the Bihar Assembly, Bihar Legislative Council, Lok Sabha, and now the Rajya Sabha. #WATCH | Nitish Kumar takes oath as a Member of the Rajya Sabha. (Video Source: Sansad TV/YouTube) pic.twitter.com/ZFbf761Wrm He was elected unopposed to the Upper House on March 16 as a candidate of the National Democratic Alliance (NDA). Shortly after, he also took over as president of the Janata Dal (United). Following his election, Kumar resigned from the Bihar Legislative Council in line with constitutional provisions that prohibit dual membership of Parliament and a state legislature. Under Article 101 of the Constitution and related rules, an elected representative must vacate one seat within 14 days. Kumar’s resignation paves the way for a bypoll to fill the vacant council seat within six months, as mandated by law. Kumar’s move to the Rajya Sabha is expected to lead to a leadership change in Bihar, with the BJP likely to play a larger role in the state government going forward. A new chief minister is expected to be decided soon, with April 14 being seen as a possible timeline. A high-level meeting of BJP leaders is underway in Delhi to finalise the roadmap for the transition. However, constitutionally, Kumar can continue as chief minister for up to six months without being a member of the state legislature. Amid speculation over leadership changes, Bihar BJP president Nitin Nabin dismissed reports of discord within the alliance. “There are no differences anywhere; everything is proceeding as per schedule… BJP has always respected gathbandhan dharma,” he told reporters. One of India’s most seasoned politicians, Nitish Kumar, began his career as an MLA in 1985 and later served as a Union minister in the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Government. He first became Bihar chief minister in 2005 and has since been a central figure in the state’s politics. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Neomile Growth Fund offloads over 1% stake in Ratnaveer Precision Engineering- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Ratnaveer Precision Engineering shares closed 1.64 percent higher at Rs 145.78 after significant volatility during the day, with high volumes.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Neomile Growth Fund, managed by Neomile Asset Managers, on April 10 sold over a percent stake in Ratnaveer Precision Engineering, the stainless steel washers manufacturer, via open market transactions. Ratnaveer Precision Engineering shares closed 1.64 percent higher at Rs 145.78 after significant volatility during the day, with high volumes. As per the bulk deals data, Neomile Growth Fund-Series I, the Category III Alternative Investment Fund, offloaded 7.24 lakh shares (1.06 percent stake) in Ratnaveer at Rs 145.01 per share, valued at Rs 10.5 crore. The fund had held 2.54 percent stake in the company as of December 2025. However, Anant Jain has acquired 10 lakh shares (1.47 percent stake) in the Ratnaveer for Rs 14.88 crore, at a price of Rs 148.8 per share. The brisk action was also seen in Medicamen Organics , Megastar Foods , Vipul, and HMA Agro Industries. SB Opportunities Fund I, which held 3.55 percent stake as of January 24, pared some stake in Medicamen Organics on Friday, offloading 78,000 shares (0.64 percent stake) at Rs 28.27 per share, valued at Rs 22.05 lakh. Singapore-incorporated variable capital company Vista Axis VCC's Quant Fund has bought 60,000 shares in Megastar Foods, the wheat products producer, at Rs 282.58 per share, valued at Rs 1.69 crore. UAE-based company Moneyplant Gold & Jewellery Trading LLC has offloaded 24 lakh shares (1.7 percent stake) in real estate firm Vipul for Rs 2.41 crore at Rs 10.06 per share, however, Bal Krishan Chhabra picked up 14.66 lakh shares (1.04 percent stake) at same price, valued at Rs 1.47 crore. Moneyplant Gold & Jewellery Trading held 6.59 percent stake in Vipul as of December 2025. Further, Moneyplant Gold & Jewellery Trading has acquired 25.05 lakh shares (0.5 percent stake) in HMA Agro Industries at Rs 23.48 per share, amounting to Rs 5.88 crore. Vipul shares closed at Rs 10.46 on the National Stock Exchange, up 1.95 percent, while HMA Agro Industries shares rallied 8.15 percent to Rs 23.75 amid significant volumes. Title: Kerala exit polls 2026 results date and time: When and where to watch the predictions?- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The LDF, led by Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan, is aiming for a rare second consecutive term in a state known for alternating governments. Its campaign has focused on its welfare record.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Voting for the Kerala assembly election was held in a single phase on Thursday, with over 2.6 crore voters heading to polling booths. They decided the fate of 883 candidates contesting across 140 constituencies. The LDF, led by Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan, is aiming for a rare second consecutive term in a state known for alternating governments. Its campaign has focused on its welfare record, while the UDF, led by VD Satheesan and Ramesh Chennithala, targeted the government over anti-incumbency and alleged governance lapses. The current assembly’s term ends on 23 May 2026, with the last election held in April 2021. According to the Election Commission, Kerala has 26,953,644 eligible voters, including 13,126,048 males, 13,827,319 females, and 277 transgender voters. Speaking after casting his vote, Vijayan said the LDF continues to enjoy public trust. “False propaganda cannot defeat the LDF… We are with the people, and the people are with us,” he said. Under Section 126A of the Representation of the People Act, 1951, conducting or publicising exit polls before polling ends is strictly prohibited. There were no exit polls on Thursday because voting would be held in multiple phases. The exit polls for the Assembly elections in West Bengal, Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry are expected to be released on the evening of April 29. Exit polls are surveys that ask voters who they voted for, along with questions about their political views, the issues that influenced their choice, and details about their background. It aims to gauge electoral trends before official results are announced. Exit polls are mostly conducted with voters as they leave a polling station during an election. In 2021, most exit polls predicted that the LDF would win 104-120 seats, whereas the Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) was predicted be reduced to 20-36 constituencies. The BJP was projected to end with 0-2 seats. In 2021, the incumbent LDF, led by the Communist Party of India (Marxist), returned to power with a historic win, securing 99 of the 140 seats. The LDF not only improved on its 2016 Assembly performance but also rebounded strongly from its weak show in the 2019 Lok Sabha elections. You can track the latest exit poll predictions and analysis on Moneycontrol.com, along with the most comprehensive Assam assembly election coverage. Additionally, exit polls will be broadcast live on CNN-News18. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Inside NASA’s strange experiment: Why ‘organ chips’ went to space with astronauts- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Tiny lab-grown replicas of human tissue are helping scientists understand what deep space really does to the body.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: When NASA sent astronauts around the Moon on Artemis II, they weren’t the only ones on board being studied. Tucked inside the spacecraft were tiny, USB-sized devices known as “organ chips.” They might sound like something out of science fiction, but the idea is actually pretty straightforward. These chips contain living human cells arranged in a way that mimics how real organs behave inside the body. What makes this experiment unusual is where those cells came from. In this case, the chips were built using bone marrow cells donated by the astronauts themselves. So instead of studying generic human tissue, scientists are effectively tracking how each astronaut’s own cells respond to space conditions, without having to take risky samples from the crew mid-mission. The goal is to answer a problem NASA has been trying to solve for years: what does deep space actually do to the human body? Low Earth orbit, where the International Space Station operates, is still relatively protected by Earth’s magnetic field. But missions like Artemis go much farther out, exposing astronauts to higher levels of radiation and longer periods in microgravity. That combination can affect everything from bones and blood cells to the immune system. These organ chips act like miniature test labs. As the spacecraft travels, researchers can study how the cells react to radiation, weightlessness and other stresses. Do they weaken? Do they repair themselves differently? Do they behave in ways that could become dangerous over longer missions? The answers matter more than they might seem. NASA isn’t just planning short trips to the Moon. It’s thinking about missions that could last months or even years, especially if humans are to travel to Mars. Understanding how the body holds up, and where it starts to break down, is critical before sending astronauts that far. Instead of waiting years for human data or exposing astronauts to unnecessary risk, these chips allow scientists to run controlled experiments in real space conditions. It’s faster, safer and far more detailed. In a way, these tiny chips are doing some of the hardest work on the mission quietly in the background, helping answer questions that will shape the future of human space travel. Title: Congress withdraws Baramati bypoll candidate after repeated requests; Sunetra Pawar set to win unopposed- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Congress state president Harshvardhan Sapkal announced that the party will withdraw its candidate. Initially, Akash More was set to contest against Pawar.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: The Congress has withdrawn its candidate from the Baramati bypoll after multiple appeals, clearing the path for NCP president and Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Sunetra Pawar to be elected unopposed. Congress state president Harshvardhan Sapkal announced that the party will withdraw its candidate. Initially, Akash More was set to contest against Deputy CM Sunetra Pawar. With polling scheduled for 23rd April, the party decided to pull back the nomination. Baramati, a key political bastion in Maharashtra, witnessed last-minute developments as opposition efforts gave way to consensus. With no rival candidates in the fray, Sunetra Pawar is now set to secure victory without a contest, marking a significant moment in the constituency’s electoral history. According to PTI, Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis called Sapkal and sought his support for an unopposed election of Sunetra Pawar. Thursday is the last day of withdrawal of nomination. Late Ajit Pawar had represented the Baramati assembly constituency, the Pawar family's home turf, eight times. Earlier on Thursday, NCP MP Supriya Sule called for an “unopposed election" in Baramati as respect and unity to honour Pawar. In a post on X, Sule wrote, “Following the untimely demise of Ajit Pawar, the Baramati Assembly constituency now faces a by-election. Ajit Dada’s political journey began with the Congress party, and he maintained a long-standing, respectful association with it throughout his career." Also, NCP leader chief Sharad Pawar also urged Sunetra’s unopposed win. "Congress has decided to contest the election. It is a national party, so we can't say anything to them. But if I am asked, I would like to advise Congress that it would be best to have an unopposed election in Baramati. The decision is up to Congress," Pawar told reporters. According to media reports, on Wednesday, Sunetra spoke with Sapkal over a phone call. This was the second instance when she spoke to the Maharashtra Congress chief since April 6, requesting him to ensure an unopposed election. Title: Assam Assembly polls see record 85.38% voter turnout- Moneycontrol.com
Description: In the Assembly elections held in 2021, Assam had recorded a total voter turnout of 82.42%.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: As Assam went to the polls on Thursday, the state recorded a voter turnout of 85.38%, the highest ever recorded in the state’s electoral history. The last time Assam saw a record voter turnout was in the 2016 Assembly elections when it recorded 84.67%. In the Assembly elections held in 2021, Assam had recorded a total voter turnout of 82.42%. Voters queued outside polling stations to cast their votes for the 722 candidates in the fray in a single-phase assembly election. Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar closely monitored polling activities through 100% live webcasting. Polling was conducted across 126 Assembly constituencies, of which 19 were reserved for Scheduled Tribes (STs) while nine were reserved for Scheduled Castes (SCs), covering an electorate of over 2.5 crore voters. Of the total electorate, 84.80 were male voters, 85.96 were female voters while 36.84 voters were from the third gender category. As per data available with the Election Commission, over 6.42 lakh voters aged between 18-19 cast their vote for the first time. To strengthen the monitoring mechanism, webcasting facilities were arranged in accordance with the Commission's directions, a release said. Webcasting was enabled in all 31,490 polling stations, including 31,486 main polling stations and four auxiliary polling stations. A total of 1,51,132 polling personnel were deployed for the conduct of the election. For polling purposes, 41,320 ballot units, 43,975 control units, and 43,997 VVPAT machines were arranged, including reserves for emergency use. The contest is largely shaped by the ruling alliance led by Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma, with the BJP-led NDA aiming to return to power for a third consecutive term. The main challenge to the NDA comes from the Congress under Gaurav Gogoi, leading the opposition campaign. Several prominent leaders such as Himanta Biswa Sarma, state Congress president Gaurav Gogoi, Leader of Opposition Debabrata Saikia, All India United Democratic Front chief Badruddin Ajmal, Raijor Dal leader Akhil Gogoi and Assam Jatiya Parishad president Lurinjyoti Gogoi are in fray. The results of the Assam Assembly elections will be declared on May 4. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: AIMIM breaks alliance with Humayun Kabir, to contest Bengal polls independently- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The party stated that it would now contest the West Bengal elections independently, stressing its commitment to giving marginalized communities an autonomous political voice.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: The All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen (AIMIM) announced on Friday that it has ended its alliance with former Trinamool Congress (TMC) leader Humayun Kabir, citing concerns over remarks attributed to him in a controversial video that has stirred political tensions ahead of the West Bengal Assembly elections. The party, led by Asaduddin Owaisi, said it “cannot associate with any statements where the integrity of Muslims is brought into question,” confirming that the tie-up with Kabir’s party has been withdrawn with immediate effect. In a statement posted on X, AIMIM said, “Humayun Kabir's revelations have shown how vulnerable Bengal's Muslims are. AIMIM cannot associate with any statements where integrity of Muslims is brought into question. As of today, AIMIM has withdrawn its alliance with Kabir's party.” Humayun Kabir’s revelations have shown how vulnerable Bengal’s Muslims are. That AIMIM cannot associate with any statements where integrity of Muslims is brought into question. As of today, AIMIM has withdrawn its alliance with Kabir’s party. Bengal’s Muslims are one of the… Reiterating its electoral strategy, AIMIM announced it will contest the upcoming Assembly elections independently, without entering into alliances. “Bengal's Muslims are one of the poorest, neglected and oppressed communities. Despite decades of secular rule, nothing has been done for them,” the party said, adding that its objective is to ensure an “independent political voice” for marginalised communities. The move marks a continuation of AIMIM’s efforts to expand beyond its traditional base in Hyderabad by directly contesting elections in multiple states. The decision comes a day after the ruling TMC circulated a purported video of Kabir, alleging his links with senior leaders of the Bharatiya Janata Party and a plan to influence the electoral outcome. According to TMC leaders, the footage suggests Kabir claimed proximity to opposition leader Suvendu Adhikari and Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma, while outlining a strategy to divert minority votes away from the ruling party. The nearly 20-minute video, which has not been independently verified, allegedly shows Kabir discussing a Rs 1,000 crore deal, including an advance of Rs 200 crore, and making controversial remarks about Muslims, along with references to using emotive issues like the Babri Masjid to mobilise voters. The TMC has demanded a probe by the Enforcement Directorate into the alleged financial transactions and broader conspiracy. The BJP has, however, rejected the charges, accusing the ruling party of diversionary tactics amid the election season. Dismissing the allegations, Kabir termed the video a “pack of lies” and claimed it was generated using artificial intelligence to malign him. “It is an attempt to malign me by the TMC, who are afraid to lose the vote of Muslims. They have insulted the community by launching such a fake video,” he said, adding that he would pursue defamation cases against Mamata Banerjee, Abhishek Banerjee and other leaders if evidence is not produced. Kabir, who was earlier expelled from the TMC, had formed his own outfit to contest the polls. Voting for the 294-member West Bengal Assembly will be held in two phases later this month on April 23 and April 29. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: 'Rushed for political mileage': Kharge writes to PM Modi, questions special session on women's quota law- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Kharge said calling a special sitting, amid ongoing state elections, reinforces the Opposition’s belief that the Centre was rushing the implementation of the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, 2023 for “political mileage” rather than genuine empowerment.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge has written to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, raising concerns over the timing of a special session of Parliament to discuss the women’s reservation law, and urging the government to convene an all-party meeting on the linked issue of delimitation. In his letter dated April 11, Kharge said calling a special sitting from April 16, amid ongoing state elections, reinforced the Opposition’s belief that the government was rushing the implementation of the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, 2023 for “political mileage” rather than genuine empowerment. “I have just received your letter on the special session of Parliament… This special sitting has been called without taking us into confidence,” Kharge wrote, adding that the government was seeking Opposition cooperation “without revealing any details on the delimitation going to be done.” Sharing my response to Modi ji, on his letter on the special session of Parliament for a discussion on the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam from the 16th of April — “As you are aware the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, 2023 was passed by Parliament unanimously in September 2023. At… pic.twitter.com/qGq95LCMmP — Mallikarjun Kharge (@kharge) April 12, 2026 He stressed that without clarity on delimitation and related constitutional changes, “it would be impossible to have any useful discussion on this historic law.” Kharge also disputed the government’s claim of prior consultations. “You mention… that your government has engaged in dialogue with political parties. However, I am pained to point out that this goes against the truth,” he said, noting that Opposition parties have been seeking an all-party meeting after April 29, once the current round of elections concludes. The Congress chief pointed out that the law, passed unanimously in September 2023, has yet to be implemented despite broad agreement at the time. “It has been 30 months since then,” he said, questioning the delay followed by what he termed a sudden push. Kharge reiterated that the proposed amendments tied to delimitation would have wide implications for both the Centre and the states and must be discussed collectively. “If the special sitting is meant to ‘strengthen our democracy’… then I would suggest that the government convene an all-party meeting any time after April 29,” he wrote. The exchange comes ahead of a three-day special session of Parliament from April 16 to 18, during which the government is expected to introduce measures to operationalise the women’s reservation law before the 2029 general elections, alongside proposals to increase Lok Sabha seats to 816, including 273 reserved for women. The Congress has also questioned the constitutional basis of linking women’s reservation to delimitation, warning of “grave consequences” if changes are pushed through without wider consultation. At a recent Congress Working Committee meeting, the party decided to reach out to other Opposition parties to evolve a joint strategy, with a meeting of senior leaders likely around April 15. Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh also criticised the timing of the session, calling it a violation of the Model Code of Conduct as elections are underway in states including Tamil Nadu and West Bengal. With polling already held in Puducherry, Assam and Kerala on April 9, and further phases scheduled on April 23 and 29 in West Bengal and April 23 in Tamil Nadu, the Opposition has argued that major legislative moves should wait until the electoral process concludes. The government, meanwhile, has maintained that discussions on the women’s reservation law are necessary to move forward on a long-pending reform, even as political differences sharpen over the process and timing. Title: Your salary went up, but you still feel broke. Here’s what’s happening- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Why higher income doesn’t always translate into more money in your account.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Getting a raise is supposed to feel like progress. You expect a little more breathing room, maybe fewer money worries, and the ability to save more. And yet, a few months later, nothing really feels different. Your salary is higher, but your bank balance doesn’t reflect it. You’re still watching your expenses, still wondering where the money goes, and still feeling like you’re just about keeping up. This isn’t unusual. In fact, it’s one of the most common financial patterns people experience. One of the first things that happens after a salary increase is subtle, almost invisible. You start ordering in a little more often. You upgrade small things — better groceries, slightly nicer clothes, more frequent cabs instead of waiting. None of these feel like big decisions, but together they raise your baseline. This is lifestyle creep. It doesn’t feel like overspending because each change is small and justified. But over time, your expenses quietly rise to match your income. The bigger impact usually comes from fixed expenses. A higher salary often leads to bigger commitments — moving to a better house, upgrading your car, taking on higher EMIs, enrolling in more expensive schools or memberships. These aren’t one-time spends. They lock you into a higher cost structure every month. And once these costs go up, they’re hard to reverse. So even though your income has increased, your flexibility hasn’t. There’s also a mindset shift that happens. Things that once felt like a stretch now feel reasonable. A slightly more expensive gadget, a more frequent holiday, a premium subscription — all justified by the idea that you’ve “earned it.” Individually, these decisions make sense. But taken together, they absorb the very raise that was supposed to give you comfort. Another reason your raise doesn’t feel as big is taxes. A higher salary often pushes you into a higher tax bracket or increases the absolute tax you pay. The number you see on your offer letter or appraisal isn’t what actually lands in your account. So while your salary may have gone up by a certain amount, your take-home increase is usually smaller than expected. As your income grows, your expectations shift with it. What once felt like a comfortable lifestyle now becomes the minimum. Your idea of what you “need” expands. So even if you’re objectively better off, it doesn’t feel that way. The gap between earning and building wealth A salary increase improves your earning capacity. It doesn’t automatically improve your financial position. That only happens if the gap between what you earn and what you spend actually widens. If your expenses rise in line with your income, that gap stays the same — and so does your sense of being financially stretched. The difference comes from how you handle the increase. If even a part of your raise is directed towards savings or investments before it gets absorbed into your lifestyle, you start to see a shift. Not immediately, but over time. Because the goal isn’t just to earn more. It’s to keep more. Feeling broke despite earning more doesn’t mean you’re doing something wrong. It usually means your financial life has adjusted faster than you realised. The key is to pause and look at where the increase went. Once you see that clearly, you can start deciding how much of your future raises you actually want to keep — instead of letting them quietly disappear. Title: Women's participation in flexi IT roles reaches 40% in FY26: Report- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Women's placements increased by 10 percentage points from FY22 to FY26, reaching 40 per cent in FY26, said talent solutions provider Careernet's report - Women in IT/ITeS: Trends in Contractual/Flexi Roles.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Placement of women in contractual or flexible roles in the IT and IT-enabled services sector has reached 40 per cent in FY26, reflecting the growing adoption of the workforce model, a report said on Saturday. Women's placements increased by 10 percentage points from FY22 to FY26, reaching 40 per cent in FY26, said talent solutions provider Careernet's report - Women in IT/ITeS: Trends in Contractual/Flexi Roles. The preferred roles tilt toward finance and accounting (43 per cent), followed by data science and analytics (34 per cent), UX, design and architecture (31 per cent), IT and information security (29 per cent) and engineering - software and QA (27 per cent), it said. Consulting (20 per cent) continues to lag, pointing to areas that have greater headroom for inclusion, added the report. Careernet's report is based on an annual sample of 3,000 placements in contractual positions in FY26 and was compared with data from the last four fiscal years. With respect to preference for geographical locations, the report found that the talent pool remains concentrated in metro hubs, with Bengaluru (21 per cent), Hyderabad (20 per cent), and Pune (17 per cent) together driving the bulk of participation. Delhi NCR (13 per cent) and Chennai (9 per cent) follow, while Mumbai (7 per cent) and Kolkata (2 per cent) contribute smaller shares, said the report. Tier II cities account for 11 per cent of women's participation, highlighting a continued concentration in established tech corridors, it said. In terms of career span, the report said that women's participation is strongest in early to mid-career stages, led by professionals with 5-8 years of experience (31 per cent), followed closely by those with less than 2 years (30 per cent) - together accounting for a significant share of the active talent pool. However, participation declined at senior levels, with 9-14 years (13 per cent) and over 14 years (4 per cent), indicating gaps in retention and advancement, it added. "Over the past five years, women's participation in IT/ITeS has evolved beyond just numbers to the kinds of roles they are choosing. Finance and accounting leads the mix, signalling that this is no longer a peripheral participation story. Women are increasingly gravitating toward functions that sit at the core of business outcomes," Careernet Chief Business Officer Neelabh Shukla said. Title: Wipro declines after IT firm announces plan to consider share buyback- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The company has secured an eight-year transformation deal with Singapore-based food and agri-business major Olam Group, which is expected to exceed about Rs 9,314 crore in contract value.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Wipro shares declined on Friday after the IT firm said it will consider a share buyback on April 16. The stock was trading at Rs 201.49 per share on the NSE, down 0.72 percent. It has been on a losing trend for the past three trading sessions. Earlier in the day, the stock had opened on a positive note, rising 2.8 percent, and touched an intra-day high of Rs 208.7, up 2.87 percent. This will be the first instance in nearly three years that the Bengaluru-based IT services company will evaluate a share buyback. Its last buyback exercise was conducted in June 2023. In that round, the company repurchased shares worth Rs 12,000 crore between June 22 and June 30, acquiring 26.96 crore equity shares, representing 4.91 percent of its total equity, at Rs 445 per share. The company’s management had earlier said during its third-quarter results that buybacks would continue to be a key route to return excess cash to shareholders. Earlier this week, the company secured an eight-year transformation deal with Singapore-based food and agri-business major Olam Group, which is expected to exceed about Rs 9,314 crore in contract value. As part of the broader engagement, Wipro said it will acquire Olam Group's IT and digital services business, Mindsprint Pte. Ltd., for about Rs 3,494 crore. "Wipro has secured a multi-year strategic transformation deal with Olam Group, a leading USD 50+ Billion Food and Agri-business headquartered in Singapore, employing nearly 40,000 people, and majority-owned by Temasek Holdings. This 8-year deal with Olam Group is expected to exceed USD 1 billion in contract value, with a committed spend of USD 800 million. "As part of this broader engagement, Wipro will acquire Mindsprint, Olam Group's IT services arm," Wipro said in an exchange filing on Monday. Title: US recurring jobless claims fall to almost two-year low- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Economists say uncertainty tied to the Iran war and higher energy costs may weigh on business sentiment, potentially leading companies to scale back hiring plans in the months ahead.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Recurring applications for US jobless benefits fell to the lowest level in almost two years, adding to evidence of stabilization in the labor market. Continuing claims, a proxy for the number of people receiving benefits, fell to 1.79 million in the week ended March 28, the least since May 2024 and below all estimates in a Bloomberg survey. Initial claims rose to 219,000 in the week ended April 4. Still, economists say uncertainty tied to the Iran war and higher energy costs may weigh on business sentiment, potentially leading companies to scale back hiring plans in the months ahead. Title: Sukanya Samriddhi Account: If a girl starts investing at 10, will she get maturity at 21 or 31? Here’s what the rule says- Moneycontrol.com
Description: To leverage these benefits, open an SSY account at a post office or authorised bank for a girl child under 10 years
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Did you know that the maturity of a Sukanya Samriddhi Account (SSA) does not depend on the age of the girl child, but on when the investment starts? This often confuses parents who open the account later, especially when the child is already 8, 9, or even 10 years old. A common question that comes to mind is if a girl starts investing at age 10, will she receive the maturity amount at 21, or will it be pushed further to 31? Adhil Shetty, CEO of Bank Bazaar, said, "The maturity of an SSA is linked to the date of opening, not the child’s age. So if the account is opened when the girl is 10, it will mature 21 years later, effectively when she turns 31, even though contributions are required only for the first 15 years." So, if the account is opened when the girl is 10, deposits can be made until she turns 25. However, this does not mean the account matures at 25 or later. The account will still mature at 21, but contributions beyond that age are not required, and in fact, deposits stop earlier as per the 15-year rule. There is another important point that many people miss. Even after the contribution period ends, the account continues to earn interest until maturity. This means the accumulated amount continues to grow over time, helping build a sizeable corpus by the time the girl turns 31. For instance, if an SSA is opened when the girl is 10 years old in 2026, and Rs 1.5 lakh is invested every year, the total contribution over 15 years comes to Rs 22.5 lakh. The scheme allows deposits only for 15 years, but the account continues to earn interest until the girl turns 31 (girl's age 10 years + 21 years SSA maturity period). In this case, the maturity year will be 2047, when she completes 31 years of age. During this full period, the invested money earns compound interest, which adds up to about Rs 49.32 lakh. So, at maturity, the total amount is Rs 71.82 lakh, including the invested amount and the interest earned over time. "The scheme, however, does provide limited liquidity at a critical stage. Once the girl turns 18, up to 50 percent of the previous year’s balance can be withdrawn, primarily for higher education expenses such as tuition or admission fees and marriage. For instance, if the balance is Rs 10 lakh at that point, up to Rs 5 lakh can be accessed, while the remaining corpus continues to compound. This balance between access and discipline is what makes SSY effective for long-term goal-based planning," said Shetty. SSA also provides significant tax advantages under Section 80C, allowing parents or legal guardians to claim a deduction of up to Rs 1.5 lakh annually on deposits made into the account, but only under the old tax regime. This deduction reduces your taxable income directly, helping families save on taxes while securing the girl child's future; the scheme's EEE status further exempts the interest earned (compounded monthly at the current rate of 8.2 percent p.a.) and maturity amount from taxation upon fulfilment of tenure conditions. "To leverage these benefits, open an SSY account at a post office or authorised bank for a girl child under 10 years, depositing a minimum of Rs 250 and up to Rs 1.5 lakh yearly until the child turns 15, with the account maturing after 21 years. The entire corpus becomes accessible at maturity- all tax-free, making it a powerful, low-risk option for long-term savings aligned with 80C limits alongside other investments like ELSS or PPF," said Chandini Anandan, Chartered Accountant at ClearTax. Title: Samsung flags eight-fold jump in Q1 profit as AI chip demand drives up prices- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The preliminary results nearly triple Samsung's previous record quarterly operating profit of 20 trillion won, reached in the October to December quarter last year.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Samsung Electronics on Tuesday projected a record-high first-quarter profit, up more than eightfold from a year earlier and well above expectations as booming demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure caused supply bottlenecks and drove chip prices higher. The world's largest memory chipmaker estimated an operating profit of 57.2 trillion won ($37.92 billion) for the January to March period, compared with an LSEG SmartEstimate of 40.6 trillion won and a more than eight-fold jump from 6.69 trillion won a year earlier. The preliminary results nearly triple Samsung's previous record quarterly operating profit of 20 trillion won, reached in the fourth quarter last year. Samsung has emerged as one of the major beneficiaries of the AI data centre boom that has constrained supply for traditional chips used in smartphones, PCs and game consoles and led to a near-doubling in chip prices in the first quarter alone. Research TrendForce expects contract DRAM memory chip prices to increase more than 50% in the current quarter as the shortage persists. About a year ago, Samsung CEO apologised for its disappointing earnings and share price performance, after the tech giant lagged its rivals in supplying high bandwidth memory (HBM) chips critical to Nvidia's AI chipsets. But Samsung has been narrowing the gap with South Korean rival SK Hynix with its latest HBM4 chips while benefiting from the rebound in traditional chip demand fueled by AI inference, which allows AI models like ChatGPT to generate responses in real time. Last month, U.S. memory chip maker Micron Technology forecast third-quarter revenue above Wall Street expectations after posting record earnings in the ​second quarter on booming AI demand and tight supply. Samsung said its revenue was expected to grow 68% to 133 trillion won in the January to March period. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Reserve Bank to ask bank boards to focus on policy, not operations- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The move follows recent tensions at HDFC Bank, the country's largest lender, where the chairman resigned abruptly, citing differences over "values and ethics."
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: The Reserve Bank of India plans to revise guidelines for bank boards to encourage greater focus on policy matters rather than day-to-day operations, Governor Sanjay Malhotra said in a monetary policy speech on Wednesday. The move follows recent tensions at HDFC Bank, the country's largest lender, where the chairman resigned abruptly, citing differences over "values and ethics." Reuters has previously reported, citing sources, that the chairman's involvement in operational and management matters had led to internal friction with CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan. "A comprehensive review of instructions is being undertaken following the request of banks, which will facilitate better utilisation of bank boards' time," Malhotra said. "We propose to revise and rationalise matters that require the attention of the board. This will result in boards being able to divert more time to policy matters, leaving operational matters to the management." Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Puducherry logs near-90% turnout in record voter participation- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Polling was conducted across 30 Assembly constituencies, of which five were reserved for Scheduled Castes (SCs), covering an electorate of over 9 lakh voters.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: As Puducherry went to the polls on Thursday, the Union Territory registered a record voter turnout of 89.83%. Voters queued outside polling stations to cast their votes in a single-phase election as the voting process started at 7am. Voting concluded at 6pm on Thursday while people who arrived before the deadline were allowed to wait in queues and cast their votes, said officials. Polling was conducted across 30 Assembly constituencies, of which five were reserved for Scheduled Castes (SCs), covering an electorate of over 9 lakh voters. Of them, 88.09% males, 91.33% females and 91.81% from the transgender category exercised their franchise. As per data available with the Election Commission, over 23,000 voters aged between 18-19 cast their vote for the first time. ECI data also showed that the last time Puducherry registered record voter turnout was in the 2011 elections when it recorded 86.19%. In the last Assembly elections held in 2021, Puducherry recorded a total voter turnout of 83.42%. The election produced a victory for the National Democratic Alliance (NDA), winning 16 seats. This time, the contest is mainly between the NDA vs Congress-DMK alliance, with smaller regional formations also in the fray. The NDA currently holds a majority and is seeking a second consecutive term. Other regional players include actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, which is independently contesting the Puducherry polls and has fielded candidates in all the 30 constituencies. A close watch is being kept on Thattanchavady, Mangalam, Lawspet, Oulgaret, Karaikal North and Yanam as these assembly constituencies will determine the future of the Union Territory. The results of the Puducherry elections will be declared on May 4. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates.
