Title: Priyadarshini Mattoo case: SC refuses to hear plea against denial of extension of convict's parole- Moneycontrol.com
Description: It is needless to say that if such a request is made, the high court shall consider it, having regard to the facts of the case that the incident occurred on January 23, 1996, and the petitioner has been in jail for 31 years, including remission, says SC bench.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: The Supreme Court on Friday refused to hear a plea challenging the denial of extension of parole granted to convict Santosh Kumar Singh in the 1996 Priyadarshini Mattoo murder case. A bench of Justices B V Nagarathna and Prashant Kumar Mishra noted that the main matter related to remission is already pending before the Delhi High Court and coming up for hearing on May 18. The bench granted liberty to Singh to seek advancement of his hearing on the plea before the high court and expeditious disposal. "It is needless to say that if such a request is made, the high court shall consider it, having regard to the facts of the case that the incident occurred on January 23, 1996, and the petitioner has been in jail for 31 years, including remission," the bench said. The top court requested the high court to dispose of the matter at the earliest in accordance with law. Counsel for Singh contended that the high court has passed a very drastic order, directing him to surrender and his client has been serving his sentence in an 'open jail', which allows him to exit the jail complex daily between 8 am and 8 pm for gainful employment. On March 19, the high court directed Singh to surrender after Mattoo's brother strongly opposed his plea for premature release. It had said that the court would consider Singh's plea for remission only after he surrendered. Mattoo's brother has contended that the sentence review board (SRB) has rightly rejected his plea for remission. Singh, who was granted parole last year, has remained out of jail after he managed to secure several extensions from the high court while his plea for remission was pending. Mattoo, 25, was raped and murdered in January 1996 and Singh, then a law student at Delhi University, was acquitted by a trial court on December 3, 1999. However, the high court overturned the verdict on October 27, 2006, convicting Singh, son of a former IPS officer, and sentencing him to death. In October 2010, the Supreme Court upheld the conviction but commuted the death sentence to life imprisonment. In July last year, the high court set aside the SRB's decision refusing the premature release of the convict, saying his reformative conduct was not considered. The high court said it found an element of reformation in Singh and referred the matter back to the SRB to consider afresh his plea for premature release. "No effort was made to evaluate the petitioner's (Singh) demonstrable reformative progress, including advanced educational qualifications, documented good conduct and participation in rehabilitation programmes," it had said. The court had noted that Singh was presently lodged in an open prison. The placement in such a prison category is a reflection of the "positive reformative conduct" of the convict and this was a critical indicator of reform which the SRB has failed to even acknowledge, let alone evaluate, it had said. The court said the SRB's decision to reject Singh's premature release plea could not be sustained as the rejection order neither disclosed a meaningful application of mind nor reflected a reasoned analysis of the reformative efforts made by him. While deciding Singh's case, the high court had said the board's holding that the convict's conduct in jail was not an indicator of what they might do outside "not only effectively nullifies the probative value of the convict's post-conviction conduct and reformative efforts" but also reflects a "patently erroneous and arbitrary approach" that undermines the very purpose of the remission policies. It had said Singh became eligible for premature release after undergoing imprisonment for 20 years, including remission. In the minutes of the SRB meetings of 2024, the board correctly acknowledged that the 2004 policy was applicable, as it was in force on the date of his conviction. In his 2023 petition before the high court, Singh had sought the quashing of the recommendation of the SRB rejecting his premature release in the October 21, 2021, meeting. Thereafter, his case was considered in a 2024 meeting and again rejected. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Political row erupts in Bengal over Humayun Kabir's purported video, TMC demands probe- Moneycontrol.com
Description: TMC state general secretary Kunal Ghosh demanded an ED probe into the matter, saying Kabir had allegedly sought a deputy chief minister post in case of a BJP victory.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: A political controversy erupted in West Bengal on Thursday after a purported video of Humayun Kabir surfaced on social media, in which he is allegedly heard claiming links with senior BJP leaders and discussing a plan to defeat Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee in the upcoming Assembly elections. The Trinamool Congress (TMC) released the alleged video at a press conference and demanded an Enforcement Directorate (ED) investigation into Kabir’s claims. The party alleged that the remarks pointed to coordination with senior BJP leaders, including Leader of Opposition Suvendu Adhikari, Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma, and officials from the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO), as part of a multi-crore plan to influence the election outcome. In the video, which has not been independently verified by PTI , Kabir is allegedly heard saying he is prepared to go to “any extent” to unseat Mamata Banerjee and claims contact with senior BJP leaders, including Adhikari. Kabir, who formed the Aam Janata Unnayan Party after his suspension from the TMC last year, is also purportedly heard saying he had communication with the PMO and was advised to coordinate with leaders in BJP-ruled states. The video further allegedly shows Kabir outlining a strategy to divert minority votes away from the ruling TMC, suggesting it could benefit the BJP. He is also heard referring to the need for financial backing worth hundreds of crores to execute such a plan across constituencies. Reacting strongly, senior TMC leader and minister Firhad Hakim said Kabir was acting in coordination with the BJP to mislead minority voters. “Don’t take the emotions and sentiment of the Muslim community for granted. This video exposes a deep-rooted conspiracy to influence the electoral outcome,” Hakim said. He also alleged that Kabir mentioned the Babri Masjid issue in the video as a tool to mobilise Muslim voters. Another senior TMC leader, minister Aroop Biswas, said the video suggested attempts to manipulate electoral outcomes through proxy actors. “The BJP is trying to manipulate the mandate by using proxy players. This video, if true, confirms our allegations,” Biswas said, also calling on the AIMIM and its chief Asaduddin Owaisi to clarify their position. TMC state general secretary Kunal Ghosh demanded an ED probe into the matter, saying Kabir had allegedly sought a deputy chief minister post in case of a BJP victory. When asked about the source of the video, Firhad Hakim said, “That is not important. What he said and his demand to be made deputy chief minister in the event of the BJP coming to power is important.” Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Largecap valuation reset: Here are funds that have delivered steady returns over 5 years- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Largecap funds are have given steady returns as valuations soften, backed by strong business fundamentals and consistent performance.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: India's largecap market is going through a shift. The recent correction has brought largecap valuations closer to long-term averages , shifting markets from expensive to more fairly priced levels. According to DSP Netra's April edition, largecap valuations have corrected to near their long-term averages around of 18 to 19 times earnings. The valuation gap between India and other emerging markets has also narrowed, making Indian equities more competitive . The correction has removed froth, shifting the market from expensive territory into fair to average valuation zones. The benchmark Nifty 50 has corrected around 13 percent this year, retreating from its 52-week high of 26,373. In this backdrop, largecap mutual funds continue to stand out for investors looking for relatively stable returns during volatile phases. These funds invest in well-established businesses with stronger balance sheets and more predictable earnings. When markets are volatile, this segment tends to hold up better than riskier categories. Moneycontrol has evaluated large-cap funds based on five-year rolling returns to find the most consistent performers. Here are the ones offering the best mix of stable growth and relative resilience: Consistent performers in the largecap category Funds like Mahindra Manulife Large Cap Fund and Nippon India Large Cap Fund lead the list but the overall spread across top performers remains tight, largely in the 15 percent to 17 percent range. The gap between the top-performing funds and the benchmark is not very wide, which highlights how competitive the largecap category is. Even the best-performing fund has delivered only modest outperformance over the Nifty, reinforcing that consistency matters more than high returns. Mahindra Manulife Large Cap Fund has delivered a 5-year rolling return of 16.94 percent, with a relatively higher concentration of about 34.5 percent in its top five holdings. The portfolio is led by large names such as as ICICI Bank , HDFC Bank and Reliance Industries, along with exposure to Bajaj Finserv and Infosys . The fund remains strongly tilted towards largecaps at nearly 89 percent, with a limited allocation to midcaps, pointing to a strategy that leans on established market leaders for stability. Nippon India Large Cap Fund has posted a 5-year rolling return of 16.68 percent, with close to 30 percent of its portfolio concentrated in its top five stocks. Its key holdings include HDFC Bank , ICICI Bank and Reliance Industries , along with Larsen & Toubro and Axis Bank . While the fund remains largely focused on large caps at over 84 percent, it has a slightly higher allocation to mid and small caps compared to peers, which can add a bit of growth potential alongside stability. ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund has delivered a 5-year rolling return of 16.33 percent, with a relatively higher concentration, about 35.5 percent, in its top five holdings. The portfolio is anchored by names such as ICICI Bank and HDFC Bank, along with Larsen & Toubro, Reliance Industries and Axis Bank. The fund has a strong a largecap tilt at around 85 percent, with limited exposure to mid and small caps, while a small portion is held in other assets, keeping the overall strategy fairly stable. Canara Robeco Large Cap Fund has posted a 5-year rolling return of 16.15 percent, with around 31 percent of its portfolio concentrated in its five stocks. Key holdings are HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank and Reliance Industries, along with State Bank of India and Larsen & Toubro. The fund maintains a strong largecap bias at close to 90 percent, with relatively lower exposure to mid caps, reflecting a focus on consistency and relatively stable performance. Baroda BNP Paribas Large Cap Fund has delivered a 5-year rolling return of 15.23 percent, with about 27 percent of its portfolio concentrated in its top five holdings, including HDFC Bank, Reliance Industries and ICICI Bank. The fund remains largely focused on largecaps, with more than 85 percent allocation, and a smaller exposure to midcaps, which keeps the overall strategy relatively stable. This data reinforces a fundamental principle of largecap investing — steady performance and disciplined portfolio construction matter more than chasing outsized returns. Title: From gaming chairs to control towers? US pitches gamers for high-stakes aviation jobs- Moneycontrol.com
Description: A new US Federal Aviation Administration campaign targets gamers, saying their skills may be surprisingly well-suited for air traffic control.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: The US is trying an unusual approach to fix a very real problem. It wants gamers to consider becoming air traffic controllers. The US Federal Aviation Administration has launched a campaign aimed directly at people who spend hours navigating complex virtual worlds. The idea is simple. If you can manage fast-moving situations on a screen, you might be able to handle real aircraft in crowded airspace. Air traffic control isn’t just another aviation job, it’s one of the toughest out there. Controllers are constantly tracking multiple aircraft, making split-second calls and staying locked in for long stretches without slipping up. According to the FAA, some people already in the role say gaming actually helped them build those instincts early on. Reacting quickly, staying calm when things get intense, and juggling multiple inputs at once are all part of both worlds. That’s exactly what this campaign is tapping into. Instead of the usual recruitment pitch, it leans on something more relatable. The idea is simple: those long hours spent gaming aren’t just timepass, they’re training your brain to deal with complexity in a very real way. Then there’s the money, which definitely gets attention. The FAA says salaries can go up to around USD 155,000, or roughly Rs 1.4 crore, within three years. For a government role, that’s a pretty big number, especially since the career path looks very different from a typical corporate job. Before anyone gets near live air traffic, they go through serious training and have to meet strict benchmarks. Even after that, it takes time to get comfortable. You’re dealing with real aircraft and real consequences, and things can change fast. There’s very little room for mistakes. In that sense, gaming isn’t being pitched as a gimmick. It’s more of a filter. The FAA is trying to identify people who are already wired to handle pressure, stay focused and think clearly when things get chaotic. The US has been facing a shortage of air traffic controllers, which has led to concerns around delays and increasing workload across airports. This campaign is one way of widening the talent pool, and maybe finding candidates in places they hadn’t seriously looked at before. Whether gamers actually make the jump remains to be seen. But the pitch is clear. If you’re already wired to handle chaos on a screen, there might be a place for you in the real world where those instincts matter even more. Title: Fisheries exports have risen from Rs 60,000 cr to Rs 68,000 cr after US tariffs: MoS Baghel- Moneycontrol.com
Description: India is exporting prawns, tuna and other fish varieties to several parts of the world, including Europe, Latin America, Middle East and Asia, he said.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: India’s fisheries exports have risen from about Rs 60,000 crore to Rs 68,000 crore as the country tapped new markets following US tariff measures, Union minister Satya Pal Singh Baghel claimed on Saturday. The government aims to achieve Rs 1 lakh crore in fisheries exports over the next five years, he said on the sidelines of the second National Veterinary, Dairy and Fisheries Symposium 2026 in Nagpur. India is exporting prawns, tuna and other fish varieties to several parts of the world, including Europe, Latin America, Middle East and Asia, he said. “India’s fisheries exports have increased by 25 per cent as we began exploring new markets soon after US tariffs were imposed,” he said at the symposium at the Maharashtra Animal and Fishery Sciences University here. However, he did not specify the period of the growth. Later, the minister told the media that Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Odisha, Lakshadweep, and the Andaman and Nicobar Islands are major fisheries export hubs in the country, covering both inland and marine production. The minister of state for fisheries, animal husbandry and dairying said that top officials from his ministry have been visiting new markets and tapping them for exports. “Our fisheries exports have risen from about Rs 60,000 crore to Rs 68,000 crore US tariff measures,” he claimed. The government is aiming to hit Rs 1 lakh crore in fisheries exports over the next five years, he said. The minister also highlighted various initiatives being taken to strengthen the fisheries, animal husbandry and dairy sectors. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Defence shares rise up to 16% amid broad-based rally; Aequs, HAL, Paras Defence lead gains- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Defence index has been on an upward trend for the past seven trading sessions and has gained over 14 percent during the period.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Defence-related shares rallied up to 16 percent on Friday amid a broad-based upmove in the domestic equity market. The Nifty India Defence index rose 1.4 percent, with 17 of its 19 constituents trading in the green. The index has been on an upward trend for the past seven trading sessions and has gained over 14 percent during the period. Aequs emerged as the top gainer, surging 16 percent. Bharat Forge and Dynamatic Technologies followed, advancing 3.43 percent and 2.9 percent, respectively. Hindustan Aeronautics , Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders and Paras Defence and Space Technologies were trading higher by 1–2 percent. According to brokerage JM Financial, shares of Hindustan Aeronautics offer a favourable risk-to-reward ratio, as the stock has declined 22 percent over the past six months compared to an 8 percent fall in the Nifty. The brokerage has set a target price of Rs 4,875 for the stock, indicating a potential upside of 21 percent. The rally in defence stocks was supported by broader market gains, as investors drew comfort from a ceasefire between the US and Iran. Title: Congress expels TN Mahila chief Hazeena Syed; she tells Alka Lamba to show ego to 'boyfriend KC Venugopal'- Moneycontrol.com
Description: In emails she shared, Syed also made personal and financial allegations, including a controversial remark telling Lamba to “go and show your ego to your boyfriend KC Venugopal, not me.”
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: A sharp internal dispute has broken out in the Congress after Tamil Nadu Mahila Congress chief M. Hazeena Syed accused senior leaders of harassment and financial wrongdoing, hours after being expelled for alleged “anti-party activities.” The expulsion order, issued on April 10 by All India Mahila Congress president Alka Lamba, said the move was taken “with immediate effect” to maintain “organisational discipline and integrity.” Syed, however, said she had already resigned earlier that day at a press conference and questioned the expulsion. “I have been humiliated… I have resigned officially… who the heck are you to send me an expel letter,” she wrote on X. @LambaAlka what kind of a saddist you are, you dont help any of us in mahila congress but harass us in the name of membership and loot money and enjoy your life, do you have any common sense i have been humiliated i have resigned officially in front of media in a press meet at… pic.twitter.com/BlxPovXNJ3 — M. Hazeena Syed (@SyedHazeena) April 10, 2026 She accused Lamba of harassing party members and misusing membership funds. “You harass us in the name of membership and loot money,” she said in her post. In emails she shared, Syed also made personal and financial allegations, including a controversial remark telling Lamba to “go and show your ego to your boyfriend KC Venugopal, not me.” She also questioned how Mahila Congress funds are used, writing, “You are living on our membership money… does this party have guts to audit AIMC account?” Syed said harassment was one of the main reasons for her resignation and warned of legal action. She said she would file an FIR over alleged “illegitimate account transaction” and “membership fraudulence,” claiming she has “legal proofs.” In her resignation letter dated April 9, Syed said her “dignity and self-respect” had been hurt and that she could not continue in her role. She said she had appealed to Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, but claimed he supported a “corrupt system” in the state unit. She also accused Tamil Nadu Congress chief Selvaperunthagai of denying her an election ticket due to “biased interest.” Despite the dispute, Syed said she would "continue to love our party". Title: Campbell Wilson steps down as Air India CEO; cites ‘right time’ for next phase in message to staff- Moneycontrol.com
Description: In his farewell note, Wilson underscored the scale of transformation undertaken since Air India’s privatisation in 2022, including the integration of four airlines and a shift from public-sector functioning to private-sector practices
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Campbell Wilson has formally announced his resignation as chief executive officer and managing director of Air India, telling employees that the airline is now ready for its “next phase” after four years of transformation under the Tata Group. In an internal message to employees, Wilson said the timing of his exit aligns with a transition point for the airline, as it prepares for large-scale aircraft inductions from 2027. “With a brief window until bulk deliveries from the nearly 600-strong aircraft orderbook commence in earnest from 2027, the time is right for me to hand over the reins for the next phase of Air India’s rise,” he wrote. Wilson added that he will remain in his role until a successor is appointed to ensure a smooth transition. Transformation journey and scale of overhaul In his farewell note, Wilson underscored the scale of transformation undertaken since Air India’s privatisation in 2022, including the integration of four airlines and a shift from public-sector functioning to private-sector practices. He highlighted a broad overhaul of leadership, workforce and operating culture, alongside system-wide modernisation, new product rollouts and enhanced service standards. The airline added over 100 aircraft to its fleet during his tenure, while the retrofit of legacy narrowbody aircraft is nearly complete and deliveries of widebody aircraft with new interiors are underway. Wilson also pointed to investments in long-term capability building, including South Asia’s largest aviation training academy, two flight simulator facilities, a flying school and a greenfield maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) base to support future growth. Even as he highlighted progress, Wilson acknowledged the challenges faced during his tenure, including global supply chain disruptions, aircraft delivery delays, geopolitical tensions, airspace closures and elevated fuel prices. He also alluded to the June 2025 crash that marked one of the most difficult periods for the airline. “We have accomplished much… especially in the face of unprecedented headwinds… and, most acutely, tragedy,” he said. Reflecting on his time at the airline, Wilson described it as a defining phase of his three-decade aviation career. “The fortitude, adaptability and positive spirit consistently shown by Air Indians… has been a highlight of my 30-year aviation career,” he said. Calling it a “true honour” to be part of the airline’s journey, he said he would continue to support Air India as it works towards becoming a “world-class global airline with an Indian heart.” Wilson’s resignation comes at a critical juncture for Air India as it balances operational challenges with an ambitious expansion strategy. The Tata Group is expected to name a successor in the coming months, with Wilson continuing in the role during the interim period to ensure continuity. Title: Bank shares snap previous day's fall to rise up to 3%; ICICI, HDFC Bank lead gains- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty advanced up to 1 percent in a broad-based rally led by financial stocks.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Bank shares on Friday rebounded from the previous session’s losses, rising up to 3 percent, as investor sentiment improved following a ceasefire between the US and Iran. The two sides are scheduled to hold negotiations in Pakistan on Saturday. Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty advanced up to 1 percent in a broad-based rally led by financial stocks. Financials, which have the highest weight on the benchmarks, have gained 8.8 percent so far this week. The Nifty PSU and the private bank index rose 1.64 percent and 2.03 percent, respectively. The Bank Nifty index, which had declined 1.5 percent in the previous session, recovered losses to rise up to 2 percent during the day. All its 14 constituents were trading in the green. ICICI Bank emerged as the top gainer in the Bank Nifty pack, rising 3.3 percent to touch an intra-day high of Rs 1,323.7 per share on the NSE. It was followed by Federal Bank and Axis Bank, which gained 2.38 percent and 2.34 percent, respectively. State Bank of India, HDFC Bank , Punjab National Bank and Kotak Mahindra Bank were trading higher by 1-2 percent. Title: As Samay Raina trends, Vir Das asks, ‘Why is no one arresting the audience?’- Moneycontrol.com
Description: As Samay Raina’s viral video ‘Still Alive’ racks up views, Vir Das questions why only comedians face legal heat, citing his own 14 cases and pointing to the role of audience reaction.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: As Samay Raina’s latest YouTube special ‘Still Alive’ racks up millions of views, a parallel conversation around stand-up comedy and legal scrutiny in India has resurfaced, this time led by Vir Das. In a recent stand-up segment that has since gone viral online, Vir Das questioned the pattern of legal action against comedians, offering a sharp take on how such controversies are perceived. He argued that while comedians may be the ones on stage, it is often the audience that amplifies the sentiment. According to him, the real “power” lies not in the jokes themselves, but in the collective response they generate. Making his point more provocatively, Das asked why legal scrutiny is directed only at performers and not at audiences who resonate with or react to the content. He suggested that the loudest truth, in many cases, comes from the crowd rather than the comic delivering the lines, hinting that authorities may be more uneasy about public sentiment than individual expression. If you’re looking to arrest comedians…do remember…. pic.twitter.com/HZIrmwq9Cy The clip quickly gained traction, sparking debate on freedom of expression and accountability in comedy. Some users pushed back, arguing that comedians, like any other citizens, are bound by the same legal framework. One commenter noted that freedom of speech does not exempt performers from consequences if boundaries are crossed, adding that accountability whether through apology or legal action is part of the system. Responding directly, Das revealed that he has personally faced multiple legal challenges over his work. He said he has fought 14 cases tied to material that addressed real issues, maintaining that such commentary should not warrant apologies. In his view, if ordinary citizens can speak about societal problems, comedians should not be treated differently for doing the same on stage. Others in the discussion defended artistic expression, arguing that comedy, like films or music, reflects societal thinking. They pointed out that audience demand plays a crucial role in shaping content, suggesting that if people continue to engage with certain kinds of humour, it is ultimately a reflection of public sentiment rather than just the performer’s intent. Title: Amit Shah's UCC promise in Bengal: Which states have implemented it?- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The UCC, envisaged under Article 44 of the Constitution, seeks to replace religion-based personal laws with a single set of rules governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Home Minister Amit Shah, on Friday, brought back into the spotlight the Uniform Civil Code (UCC), promising its implementation in West Bengal within six months if the BJP forms the government. The commitment, part of the party’s 2026 Assembly election manifesto, comes as a few states have already moved to adopt a uniform civil law framework. The UCC, envisaged under Article 44 of the Constitution, seeks to replace religion-based personal laws with a single set of rules governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption. Despite being a long-standing constitutional goal, its implementation has remained politically contentious for decades. So far, Uttarakhand and Gujarat have enacted UCC laws in recent years. Uttarakhand became the first state in independent India to pass the legislation in February 2024, with the law coming into force a year later, on January 27, 2025. According to Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami, the move was aimed at ensuring equality across communities. “UCC will bring uniformity in society and ensure equal rights and responsibilities for all citizens,” he said while announcing its rollout. The Uttarakhand law standardises personal civil matters by mandating registration of marriages and live-in relationships, banning practices such as polygamy and triple talaq, and ensuring equal inheritance rights. It, however, excludes certain tribal communities, reflecting the legal complexities surrounding uniformity. Gujarat followed next, with the Assembly passing the UCC Bill in March this year, after a marathon debate lasting over seven hours. The legislation, introduced by Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel, makes Gujarat the second state to adopt such a framework. Backing the move, Shah termed it a “historic step” and said, “It has been the BJP’s resolve since its inception that there should be a uniform law for every citizen in the country.” The Gujarat law similarly seeks to unify rules governing marriage, divorce, inheritance and live-in relationships across communities, though opposition parties had pushed for greater scrutiny before its passage. Beyond these two states, Madhya Pradesh has initiated the process to bring in a UCC. Earlier this week, Chief Minister Mohan Yadav directed officials to begin drafting the law, with the state targeting implementation by the end of 2026. The proposed framework is expected to draw on the experiences of Uttarakhand and Gujarat. The UCC remains one of the BJP’s key ideological commitments, alongside the abrogation of Article 370 in 2019 and the Ram Temple construction in Ayodhya, both of which have already been achieved since the party came to power at the Centre in 2014. Title: 'Causing her more harm': Elderly woman speaks to AI ‘son’ without knowing he died in accident- Moneycontrol.com
Description: A family used artificial intelligence to create a digital version of a man who died in a road accident, so his elderly mother could continue interacting with him.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: After a fatal accident in China last year, a grieving family used artificial intelligence to recreate a digital avatar to support their elderly mother emotionally. According to the SCMP , the digital creation interacts with his elderly mother, offering comfort as she comes to terms with her only son’s death. The incident was revealed by the AI team leader Zhang Zewei, who is based in eastern Jiangsu province, Litchi News reported. The family, based in Shandong province in eastern China, is among Zhang’s clients. Early last year, when the news of the mother’s only child's death broke, the family tried to hide it from his mother, who is in her 80s and suffering from a health disease. The man’s son approached Zhang, requesting a digital twin of his father using multiple photos, videos and audio clips of him speaking in his dialect. According to the reports, the AI creation looks exactly like the deceased man and even replicates his habit of leaning forward while speaking. The virtual son now speaks to the mother every day through video call on a chat app. The mother often reminds the “son” to eat his meal properly, wear warm clothes and take care of himself outdoors. In response, the digital son assures her that he will follow her advice, adding that he is working in another city to earn money and cannot return to stay with her. “You should call me more often so that I know whether you live well or not in another city. I am missing you so much. I feel so sorry that I cannot see you in person,” said the mother in one conversation. The son replied, “OK, mum. But I am too busy. I cannot talk to you for a long time. You take care of yourself. When I have made enough money, I will return home to pay my filial piety to you.” So far, the mother remains unaware of her son’s health, and the report did not reveal further details about the family. Zhang, who has been providing the AI service for three years, jokingly refers to himself as “deceiving people’s emotions”. The story drew massive attention online in mainland China, where netizens expressed mixed views. One user wrote, “It is very touching. The AI son is a gentle lie.” Another person said, “I do not side with this family. The mother has been cheated for a long time. I am afraid once the truth comes to light, it will cause her more harm.” Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: ‘If you're stuck…’: Google techie quits after 6 years, calls it best decision despite pay cut- Moneycontrol.com
Description: An Google techie quit after six years, took a pay cut, and said the move improved his happiness as he chose meaningful work over corporate pressure, sparking debate on mental health and big company culture.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: An ex-Google techie said quitting the giant company after six long years was the best decision of his life, even though it came with a pay cut. The experience was shared on LinkedIn by Sujoy Ghosh, who highlighted how the techie chose better mental health and meaningful work over a brand value and a high salary. In the post, Ghosh mentions that while the techie now earns less, he feels happier as he is no longer under constant corporate performance pressure and deadlines. He isn't trapped in the promotion games and office politics; rather, he focuses on quality work. “Now he's making less money, but he is happy because he is delivering meaningful work. That corporate performance pressure is gone,” Ghosh said on LinkedIn. Ghosh pointed out that this is a common pattern where employees often find themselves stuck in stressful work environments just for the sake of a well-known company name on their resume. In the process, they deal with office politics, chase promotions, and compromise their mental health. He further highlighted that many talented professionals in big tech companies eventually stop learning and building skills and instead get caught up in internal tasks.” The post sparked widespread discussion among users. One user said, “In the same boat can understand where this is stemming from !!” Another person wrote, “It's not always easy. I stayed stuck in MS for 8 years doing something that wasn't pleasant to me. I could not move due to various reasons. The big companies do teach things. PPT making is a useful skill. At the same time, life happens. We cannot always choose our work or the circumstances. It's always a luck factor. Destiny guides us. We chose nothing. So worry not. Follow destiny. It will take you where it wants.” A third user added, “Many users said prioritising mental health over a big brand name is a brave and necessary choice.” Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Why chasing returns can hurt more than it helps- Moneycontrol.com
Description: How to structure your retirement portfolio so it protects your money today while still growing enough to keep up with inflation over time.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: The temptation to stay heavily invested in equities doesn’t really go away in retirement. After all, higher returns sound like the obvious solution. Markets don’t move in a straight line, and in retirement, you don’t have the luxury of waiting out a bad phase. If the market falls sharply in the first few years — when you’re also withdrawing money — the damage can be hard to recover from. This is what’s known as sequence risk. It’s not just about how markets perform, but when they perform. And unfortunately, it’s one of the risks people don’t think about enough. It’s not safety vs returns — you need both Most people frame this as a choice: play safe or aim for growth. In reality, retirement planning works better when you stop thinking in extremes. Different parts of your money need to do different things. One part should be completely safe and easy to access — enough to cover your expenses for the next two to three years. This is your cushion. It could sit in bank deposits, liquid funds or similar low-risk options. The idea is simple: you shouldn’t be forced to sell investments when markets are down. Then comes the income layer — money that’s relatively stable but still working for you. Instruments like the Senior Citizens' Savings Scheme, Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana or high-quality debt funds fit in here. This is what helps you meet regular expenses without worrying about market swings. And finally, there’s the growth layer. Even in retirement, you need some exposure to equities or equity mutual funds. Not for quick gains, but to make sure your money keeps pace with inflation over time. One way to make this more practical is to stop treating your money as one big pool. Instead, break it into buckets based on when you’ll need it. The first bucket is for immediate expenses — the next couple of years. The second is for the medium term, providing stability and income. The third is for the long term, where growth matters more than short-term volatility. What this does is take away the constant pressure of market movements. You’re not reacting to every dip because your near-term needs are already taken care of. In retirement, income matters more than returns During your working years, the focus is on growing your wealth. In retirement, it’s about turning that wealth into a steady, reliable income. A slightly lower but predictable return often works better than chasing higher returns that come with uncertainty. Because at the end of the day, what matters is whether your portfolio can support your monthly expenses without stress. This is also why withdrawal planning matters. Taking out too much, especially in the early years, can quietly strain your portfolio — particularly if markets aren’t doing well. Why rebalancing quietly does the heavy lifting Over time, your portfolio won’t stay where you set it. If markets do well, your equity exposure will creep up. If they fall, it will shrink. Either way, your original balance gets disturbed. Rebalancing is simply about bringing things back to where they should be. It sounds basic, but it forces discipline — booking gains when markets are high and not letting risk build up unnoticed. Keep it simple — it actually works better It’s easy to overcomplicate retirement investing, especially with so many products available. But in reality, a simpler structure tends to work better. Fewer instruments, clearly defined roles, and a setup you understand well — that’s what makes a portfolio manageable over time. Because the goal here isn’t to optimise every last percentage point. It’s to make sure your money keeps working without constant intervention. There isn’t a perfect formula for balancing safety and returns in retirement. It’s more about getting the mix right for your life — enough safety to handle the present comfortably, and enough growth to make sure you’re not falling behind in the future. When that balance is in place, your portfolio stops feeling like something you need to constantly worry about, and starts doing what it’s meant to do — quietly support you. Title: What happens if you stop contributing to NPS midway- Moneycontrol.com
Description: You don’t lose what you’ve built, but the way it grows and what you can do with it changes more than most people expect
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: It’s quite common to start contributing to the National Pension System with good intent, and then at some point pause or stop, sometimes because cash flow gets tighter, sometimes because priorities shift, and sometimes simply because it slips out of focus. The first thing to know is that your account doesn’t disappear if you stop contributing. Whatever you’ve already invested stays invested and continues to move with the market, so in that sense, nothing is lost. But the experience of that money changes once contributions stop. Your account stays, but it becomes inactive If you don’t contribute the minimum required amount in a financial year, your NPS account gets marked as inactive, which doesn’t mean it’s closed, but it does mean you won’t be able to operate it freely until it’s reactivated. Reactivation is usually straightforward, you can contribute the minimum amount along with a small penalty, but until you do that, the account sits there without new inflows. Your money continues, but without fresh momentum The money you’ve already invested stays where it is and continues to move with the market, whether that’s equity, corporate bonds or government securities based on your allocation, so it’s not like things come to a halt. But what does change is the pace at which your corpus grows, because without fresh contributions coming in regularly, that steady compounding effect slows down, and over time the gap between what could have been and what actually is starts becoming noticeable. You lose the rhythm more than anything else One of the biggest benefits of NPS isn’t just the returns, it’s the habit of putting money aside consistently without overthinking it. Once you stop, it’s not just about missing a year or two of contributions, it’s that the rhythm breaks, and restarting later often feels harder than it should, especially when other expenses and priorities have already filled that space. A part of the appeal of NPS is the tax benefit it offers under sections like 80CCD(1) and 80CCD(1B), which makes those yearly contributions feel a bit more efficient. The moment you stop contributing, that benefit goes away as well, so you’re not just missing out on investment growth, but also on a tax break that would have come along with it. Even if you stop investing, the nature of the product doesn’t change. It’s still meant for retirement, which means you can’t freely access the full amount whenever you want, and at exit, a portion still needs to go into an annuity, regardless of whether you contributed consistently or stopped midway. Restarting sounds simple, but isn’t always Technically, you can restart anytime by reactivating your account and making the required contribution, so on paper it looks easy. In reality, once there’s been a gap, getting back into it can take more effort than expected, especially if your financial situation or priorities have shifted in the meantime, and those missed years don’t really get compensated for later. Stopping midway doesn’t undo what you’ve already built, and nothing dramatic happens overnight, which is exactly why it feels harmless at the time. But over the long term, that pause shows up as slower growth, missed contributions and lost time, and the difference between staying consistent and stopping midway ends up being much larger than it first appears. Title: Supreme Court to hear pleas against SIR electoral roll freeze in Bengal on April 13- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The poll panel had frozen and finalised the electoral rolls on April 9 for constituencies going to polls in the first phase, effectively barring inclusion of any new or previously deleted voters for this election cycle.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: The Supreme Court of India, on Friday, agreed to hear on April 13 a fresh plea, along with pending petitions, challenging the freezing of electoral rolls by the Election Commission of India ahead of the upcoming assembly elections in West Bengal. The poll panel had frozen and finalised the electoral rolls on April 9 for constituencies going to polls in the first phase, effectively barring inclusion of any new or previously deleted voters for this election cycle. A bench comprising Chief Justice Surya Kant and justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M Pancholi was urged to take up the matter urgently, with a lawyer pointing out that several appeals against the deletion of names from the voters’ list remain pending. “We will consider the petition on April 13,” the Chief Justice said. Senior advocate DS Naidu, appearing for the Election Commission, maintained that April 9 was the cut-off date for freezing the rolls. “After that, none will be considered. Right to vote remains, these appellants are similarly situated like others whose appeals were allowed,” he said. During the hearing, Justice Bagchi underscored the broader constitutional dimensions of the issue. “There is a cut-off line with respect to an election, and underlying is a constitutional right to be on the electoral roll and to vote in further elections. That is much higher and permanent,” he observed. The Chief Justice, however, noted that affected individuals are not being permanently deprived of their voting rights. Earlier, on April 6, the top court had observed that nearly 60 lakh claims and objections arising from deletions during the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls had already been decided. The bench had also directed the Chief Justice of the Calcutta High Court to set up a three-member panel of former judges to evolve uniform procedures for 19 tribunals handling appeals related to voter list deletions. Assembly elections in West Bengal are scheduled to be held in two phases on April 23 and April 29, with counting of votes on May 4. The outcome of the April 13 hearing could have significant implications for voters excluded from the rolls ahead of the polls. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Stock in Focus: 13 अप्रैल को इस स्टॉक पर रहेगा फोकस, सिर्फ तीन दिन और, फिर आ जाएगी ₹30 के डिविडेंड की एक्स-डेट
Description: Stock in Focus: यहां एक ऐसे स्टॉक की डिटेल दी जा रही है, जिसके प्रति शेयर ₹30 के डिविडेंड की एक्स-डेट जल्द आने वाली है। अगर यह स्टॉक आपके पोर्टफोलियो में अभी नहीं है तो इस डिविडेंड को हासिल कने के लिए अब सिर्फ तीन ही दिनों का मौका है। डिटेल्स में पढ़ें
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Stock in Focus: गोल्ड लोन बांटने वाली देश की सबसे बड़ी NBFC (नॉन-बैंकिंग फाइनेंशियल फाइनेंशियल) मुथूट फाइनेंस (Muthoot Finance) के शेयरों में 13 अप्रैल से शुरू होने वाले कारोबारी हफ्ते में जोरदार हलचल दिख सकती है। इसकी वजह ये है कि मुथूट फाइनेंस हर शेयर पर ₹30 का डिविडेंड बांट रही है जिसके लिए एक्स-डेट 13 अप्रैल है। इसका मतलब हुआ कि अगर यह डिविडेंड चाहिए तो 13 अप्रैल से पहले तक इसे खरीदना होगा और चूंकि 14 अप्रैल मंगलवार को डॉ बाबा साहेब आंबेडकर जयंती के मौके पर स्टॉक मार्केट में कारोबार बंद रहेगा तो इस डिविडेंड के लिए मुथूट फाइनेंस के शेयरों की खरीदारी के लिए सिर्फ तीन ही दिनों का मौका है। अभी कैसी है Muthoot Finance के शेयरों की स्थिति? मुथूट फाइनेंस के शेयरों ने निवेशकों की ताबड़तोड़ कमाई कराई है। पिछले साल 11 अप्रैल 2025 को बीएसई पर यह ₹1964.35 के भाव पर था जो इसके शेयरों के लिए एक साल का रिकॉर्ड निचला स्तर है। इस निचले स्तर से 9 ही महीने में यह 111.21% उछलकर 29 जनवरी 2026 को ₹4149.00 पर पहुंच गया जो इसके शेयरों के लिए एक साल का रिकॉर्ड हाई लेवल है। इस प्रकार महज 9 महीने में इसने निवेशकों के पैसों को डबल से अधिक कर दिया। हालांकि शेयरों की तेजी यहीं थम गई और फिलहाल इस हाई से यह 13.89% डाउनसाइड है। मुथूट फाइनेंस ने 12 फरवरी को दिसंबर 2025 तिमाही के कारोबारी नतीजे पेश किए जिसमें सामने आया कि पिछले वित्त वर्ष 2026 की तीसरी तिमाही कंपनी के लिए धमाकेदार रही। मुथूट फाइनेंस का शुद्ध मुनाफा सालावना आधार पर 94.9% उछलकर ₹2656 करोड़ पर पहुंच गया। इस दौरान एनबीएफसी की नेट इंटेरेस्ट इनकम (NII) भी 64% चढ़कर ₹4467 करोड़ पर पहुंच गया। दिसंबर 2025 तिमाही के आखिरी में कंपनी का कंसालिडेटेड लोन एयूएम सालाना आधार पर ₹1,11,308 करोड़ से 48% बढ़कर ₹1,64,720 करोड़ पर पहुंच गया। गोल्ड लोन एयूएम भी 9 महीने में सालाना आधार पर 50% बढ़कर दिसंबर 2025 के आखिरी में ₹1,39,658 करोड़ पर पहुंच गया। Nifty में 7% की रिकवरी, बीत गया मार्केट का बुरा दौर? एक्सपर्ट ने दो सेक्टर्स पर लगाया दांव तो एक से बनाई दूरी IPO Listing: 13 अप्रैल को इन दो स्टॉक्स की लिस्टिंग, ग्रे मार्केट से मिल रहे ये संकेत Madhusudan Kela ने एक साल के निचले स्तर पर लगाया दांव, 2% से अधिक खरीद ली होल्डिंग, आपके पास है शेयर? डिस्क्लेमर: यहां मुहैया जानकारी सिर्फ सूचना के लिए दी जा रही है। यहां बताना जरूरी है कि मार्केट में निवेश बाजार जोखिमों के अधीन है। निवेशक के तौर पर पैसा लगाने से पहले हमेशा एक्सपर्ट से सलाह लें। मनीकंट्रोल की तरफ से किसी को भी पैसा लगाने की यहां कभी भी सलाह नहीं दी जाती है। हिंदी में शेयर बाजार , स्टॉक मार्केट न्यूज़ , बिजनेस न्यूज़ , पर्सनल फाइनेंस और अन्य देश से जुड़ी खबरें सबसे पहले मनीकंट्रोल हिंदी पर पढ़ें. डेली मार्केट अपडेट के लिए Moneycontrol App डाउनलोड करें।

Title: SEBI extends broad-based fund rules to AIFs managed by AMCs and subsidiaries- Moneycontrol.com
Description: SEBI said that AIFs qualify as pooled assets under the regulatory framework.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Market regulator Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has clarified that asset management companies (AMCs) and their subsidiaries that provide management or advisory services to alternative investment funds (AIFs) must comply with the “broad-based fund” requirement, thereby extending the applicability of mutual fund norms to such pooled investment vehicles. In an informal guidance issued on April 9 in response to a query from UTI Alternatives Pvt Ltd, SEBI said that AIFs qualify as pooled assets under the regulatory framework. Accordingly, the condition requiring a fund to have at least 20 investors, with no single investor holding more than 25 percent of the corpus, will apply to AIFs and their schemes when managed or advised by AMCs or their subsidiaries. The regulator further clarified that compliance with the broad-based criteria must be assessed at the level of individual schemes rather than at the fund level. Under AIF regulations, each scheme is treated as a distinct investment vehicle for investor limits, corpus requirements, and compliance obligations. SEBI’s informal guidance letter stated," the broad-based requirement shall be assessed at the scheme level, as it reflects the actual investor composition of the pooled vehicle to which management/advisory services are rendered by the AMC or its subsidiaries". Addressing structures involving master and feeder funds, SEBI said that each fund—whether master or feeder—must independently meet the broad-based requirement. This is irrespective of whether investment decisions are taken at the master fund level, indicating that no blanket relaxation is available for feeder structures. Also read: SEBI crackdown in RRP Semiconductor case: Rajendra K Chodankar among 39 restrained On the question of exemptions, SEBI noted that certain relaxations available to specified categories of foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) under mutual fund regulations do not extend to domestic regulated entities. Banks, insurance companies, and provident fund trusts, being governed by separate domestic frameworks, would not qualify for such exemptions. The regulator emphasised that the guidance is based on the specific facts presented and reflects the department’s interpretative position. It does not constitute a binding decision of the Board and may vary with different circumstances. Title: OpenAI accuses Elon Musk of ‘ambush’ as $100 billion-plus trial looms- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Musk sued OpenAI and Microsoft Corp. in 2024 over claims that the maker of ChatGPT abandoned its founding mission as a research institution when it took billions of dollars in backing from the software stalwart and planned its restructuring as a for-profit business. A trial over those allegations is set for April 27. OpenAI and Microsoft have denied wrongdoing.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: OpenAI says Elon Musk has suddenly changed direction on what he’s seeking in his lawsuit against the startup in a “legal ambush” just weeks before trial. In a court filing late Friday night, OpenAI said the objectives Musk proposed earlier in the week appear to be aimed at “sandbagging the defendants and injecting chaos into the proceedings, while trying to recast his public narrative about his lawsuit.” Musk sued OpenAI and Microsoft Corp. in 2024 over claims that the maker of ChatGPT abandoned its founding mission as a research institution when it took billions of dollars in backing from the software stalwart and planned its restructuring as a for-profit business. A trial over those allegations is set for April 27. OpenAI and Microsoft have denied wrongdoing. The world’s richest person told the court in January he was seeking in the range of $79 billion to $134 billion in “wrongful gains” from his adversaries, based in large part on how much the value of OpenAI has exploded since Musk worked with Sam Altman and others to found the startup in 2015. A judgment of that magnitude would be among the largest court awards in US history. Then Musk’s lawyers said in a filing this week that any money he wins at trial should go back to OpenAI instead of to him. They also said Musk wants the court to unwind OpenAI’s conversion and to oversee its financings and transactions in the future ensure it doesn’t stray from its original mission — and for Altman to be ousted from his leadership roles as OpenAI’s chief executive officer and board member. OpenAI said in Friday’s filing that these 11th-hour proposals are “legally improper and factually unsupported.” “Musk’s proposed amendment would require the presentation of different evidence and different witnesses than the case he sponsored until three days ago,” OpenAI’s lawyers wrote. Title: Momentum Stocks: Onyx Biotec, New India Assurance rally; Info Edge, APL Apollo see negative breakdowns- Moneycontrol.com
Description: In a market driven by technical cues, several stocks witnessed sharp price action on April 10, with some moving above key moving averages, signalling strengthening momentum, while others slipped below important levels, indicating weakness.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: In a market driven by technical cues, several stocks witnessed sharp price action on April 10, with some moving above key moving averages, signalling strengthening momentum, while others slipped below important levels, indicating weakness. Among the positive breakouts, Onyx Biotec surged 13.33 percent to Rs 38.00, trading above its 30-day and 50-day moving averages. New India Assurance jumped 10.84 percent to Rs 148.61, crossing above its 30-day and 50-day moving averages, indicating improving near-term sentiment. Sylvan Plyboard rose 9.42 percent to Rs 51.00, trading above its key short-term averages, while Hind Rectifiers advanced 7.13 percent to Rs 765.10, moving above both short-term and long-term moving averages, signalling strong momentum. Nectar Lifesciences gained 6.24 percent to Rs 11.75, trading above its 30-day average, though it remains below longer-term levels. On the downside, several stocks witnessed negative breakouts, slipping below key moving averages and reflecting selling pressure. Info Edge declined 0.89 percent to Rs 985.00, trading below its 30-day and 50-day moving averages and remaining well below its longer-term averages. APL Apollo fell 0.64 percent to Rs 2,026.40, slipping below its short-term averages, though it continues to hold above its long-term levels. Welspun Investments edged lower by 0.33 percent to Rs 1,272.40, trading below its 30-day and 50-day averages. Le Travenues Technology declined 0.29 percent to Rs 169.09, falling below key averages and remaining under pressure. LCC Infotech slipped 0.21 percent to Rs 4.83, trading below its short-term averages, indicating weak momentum. The divergence in price action highlights a stock-specific market, with traders closely tracking technical levels for cues on further direction, as breakouts above moving averages signal strengthening trends, while breakdowns point to continued weakness. Title: MC Insider: A reset at the very top and a lender that says no to loans- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Track the drama, deals and decision-making playing out away from the public eye.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: The odds are stacking up against a prominent name in the private banking space. And no, before the wild guessing begins, this isn’t about the bank that recently saw a boardroom spat. This one belongs to the “big seven”, a lender that hasn’t quite been on the right side of things for a while now. There have been a few inquiries, some senior exits, and attempts to steady the ship. But market watchers say the overhang hasn’t fully lifted. Now, word is that at a recent meeting of the country’s top heads, a consensus seems to have emerged that a certain entity should no longer be associated with the bank. Does this mean a change is around the corner? Nothing is official yet. But one expects something to happen in the coming months. In a rather peculiar situation, and perhaps the first time since Covid, a top private bank is said to have halted its loan disbursements for almost a week. The bank did not sign off on any loans as it was focused on getting its deposit engine right. We understand that it happened at one of the large banks, as a very critical loan-to-deposit metric shot off the roof rather unexpectedly. Desperate times call for desperate measures, and notwithstanding the fact that it was the critical year-end quarter, the bank decided to halt its asset side of the business just to restore some sanity to its liabilities, aka deposits. Seems like the fight for deposits is nowhere close to ending.

Title: Khera is a 'bhagoda': Assam CM Sarma to Congress allegation on his wife holding three passports- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The CM's response came after the Congress shared a self-made video of Pawan Khera from an "undisclosed location" levelling allegations of corruption against Sarma.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma on Thursday hit out at Congress spokesperson Pawan Khera, calling him a "bhagora" (absconder). "Where is this Congress party? Which Congress party? I have not even heard about it. Who is Pawan Khera? He is a 'bhagora' (absconder). Why do I need to reply to him?" said CM Sarma. His remarks come in the backdrop of fresh allegations levelled by Pawan Khera against CM Sarma amidst the ongoing passport row. Meanwhile, on Wednesday, in a post on X, the Congress shared a self-made video of Khera from an "undisclosed location" levelling allegations of corruption against Sarma. Questioning the Assam CM, the party wrote, "If you have the guts, answer them." In the video, Khera criticised the police action against him and demanded answers to his questions. Calling for an investigation into the matter, the Congress leader said that he won't be scared. The remarks were followed by Congress' claims that the Assam Chief Minister's wife holds three passports, and Assam Police searched Pawan Khera's residence in Delhi. The searches followed the FIR lodged by Sarma's wife, Riniki Bhuyan Sharma, into the matter on Monday. Earlier, addressing a press conference in Guwahati on Monday, Sarma vowed to take "stringent action" against Pawan Khera. "I will take stringent action against Congress leader Pawan Khera. Just wait for a few days. We will win at least 100 seats in the elections," the CM said. CM Sarma further countered the allegations levelled by Congress, claiming that property documents used to link his family to apartments in Dubai were stolen from the document-sharing platform Scribd. He asserted that the Congress is "finished" in the state, while reiterating his allegations of Pakistani links in the ongoing passport row involving Congress leaders Gaurav Gogoi and Pawan Khera over claims related to his wife, Riniki Bhuyan Sharma. This comes as voting begins in Assam. The voter turnout at 01:30 pm on Thursday in Assam was recorded at 59.6 per cent, as per the Election Commission of India. Voters queued outside polling stations to cast their votes in a single-phase assembly election. In Assam, more than 2.5 crore electors are eligible to exercise their franchise for the 126-member legislative assembly. There are 722 candidates in the fray. Of 2,50,54,463 electors, 1,25,31,552 are male voters and 1,25,22,593 females. There are 318 voters in the Transgender category and 63,423 service voters. Among the electorate, 6,42,314 voters are in the age group of 18-19 years, 2,50,006 are above 80 years of age, and 2,05,085 are persons with disabilities. To strengthen the monitoring mechanism, webcasting facilities have been arranged in accordance with the Commission's directions, a release said. Webcasting has been enabled in all 31,490 polling stations, including 31,486 main polling stations and 4 auxiliary polling stations. A total of 1,51,132 polling personnel have been deployed for the conduct of the election. For polling purposes, 41,320 Ballot Units, 43,975 Control Units, and 43,997 VVPAT machines have been arranged, including reserves for emergency use. Additional polling personnel have also been kept in readiness, if required. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: Congress unveils Tamil Nadu manifesto, promises Universal Basic Income and 3 lakh jobs- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Public safety is a major point in their manifesto, with plans to recruit 40,000 new police personnel. which will comprise 10,000 women commandos.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: In the run-up to the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections on April 23, the Congress on Thursday released its manifesto aimed at "Transforming Tamil Nadu". Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister DK Shivakumar, Congress MP Karti Chidambaram, and State Congress chief K Selvaperunthagai were present during the unveiling. The manifesto pledges to transform the state with major initiatives targeting employment, public safety, healthcare, and law and order. One of the highlights of the election document is the promise of Universal Basic Income (UBI). The party has pledged Rs 2,000 per month for every family, with direct cash transfers into the bank accounts of women. The scheme will cover all Ration Card holders across the state. The Congress has also set a strict timeline for tackling unemployment. Their "3 Lakh Govt. Jobs in 300 Days" initiative promise includes filling all vacant positions across government departments and publishing department-wise vacancy lists for transparency. They will also create a transparent recruitment calendar for better clarity about jobs. Public safety is a major point in their manifesto, with plans to recruit 40,000 new police personnel. which will comprise 10,000 women commandos to be inducted into the force. The party also pledged to build a world-class call centre integrating police, fire, child helpline, and ambulance services with "Dial 100" as the unified number for all emergencies. The party shared their aim to position Tamil Nadu as a Global Spiritual Tourist Centre by promoting historical places of worship, offering multilingual guides, and heritage tours. On the health front, the party promises a "Rabies Free TN" with 100% dog vaccination and sterilisation by 2030, alongside an infrastructure push for pothole-free roads and a "Near Zero Road Accident Deaths" target. Tamil Nadu will go to the polls in a single phase on April 23, covering a total of 234 constituencies in the State. The counting of votes is scheduled for May 4. The main contest is expected between the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance (SPA), which includes the Indian National Congress, DMDK and VCK, and the National Democratic Alliance (NDA), led by AIADMK with BJP and PMK as allies. The Congress is contesting 28 seats as part of its alliance. Actor-turned-politician Vijay is also set to make his electoral debut with TVK, which could turn the contest into a three-way fight. Title: Are we really alone? NASA chief says the odds don’t look that way- Moneycontrol.com
Description: With trillions of galaxies and growing discoveries, the search for life beyond Earth is starting to feel less like science fiction.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: NASA chief Jared Isaacman has said what a lot of scientists have been hinting at for years. The chances that we are alone in the universe are probably not that high. Speaking about the possibility of alien life, Isaacman pointed to the sheer scale of the universe. There are an estimated two trillion galaxies out there. Each galaxy contains billions of stars, and many of those stars have planets orbiting them. When you start looking at the numbers like that, the idea that Earth is the only place where life exists begins to feel unlikely. He put it simply. At some point, we are likely to find something that suggests we are not alone. That doesn’t mean scientists expect to discover little green creatures anytime soon. The more realistic expectation is much simpler. Microbial life, basic forms of organisms, or chemical signs that point to biological activity somewhere beyond Earth. This is what a lot of space missions are actually trying to figure out right now. NASA isn’t just looking into deep space randomly. It’s focusing on places that might realistically support life. Mars has been the obvious one for years, with rovers digging around for signs that simple life may have existed there in the past. But it’s not just Mars anymore. Moons like Europa and Enceladus have become just as interesting, mainly because scientists believe there could be oceans hidden under their icy surfaces. And then there’s everything beyond our solar system. In the last few years, telescopes have been spotting planets around distant stars at a much faster pace. Some of these are in what scientists call the habitable zone, basically not too hot, not too cold, where liquid water could exist. That matters because, as far as we know, water is a big part of how life starts. Isaacman’s point is that this isn’t some side project NASA works on when it has time. This is a core question. A lot of what gets studied, where missions are sent, and how money is spent ties back to trying to answer one thing. Are we actually alone, or not? At the same time, there’s still no direct evidence of life beyond Earth. Not yet. But the way scientists look at it, the absence of proof so far doesn’t mean absence of life. It may just mean we’re still early in the search. And given the scale of the universe, that search could take time. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: 100% road tax waiver for electric cars priced up to Rs 30 lakh till Mar 2030: Delhi's draft EV policy- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The draft Delhi Electric Vehicle Policy 2026-2030, uploaded on the transport department's website, also proposes a 50 per cent exemption for strong hybrid EVs
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: All electric cars with an ex-showroom price of Rs 30 lakh or less registered in the national capital will be granted 100 per cent exemption on road tax and registration fees till 31 March 2030, according to the Delhi government's new draft electric vehicle (EV) policy released on Saturday. The draft Delhi Electric Vehicle Policy 2026-2030, uploaded on the transport department's website, also proposes a 50 per cent exemption for strong hybrid EVs. "Electric cars with ex-showroom price above (Rs) 30 lakh registered in Delhi shall not be granted any exemption from road tax and registration fees," the draft stated. From January 1, 2027, new registrations of only electric three-wheelers shall be permitted in Delhi, it said. "The Delhi Electric Vehicle Policy 2026-2030 builds on this momentum to accelerate electric vehicle adoption, improve air quality and create a supportive ecosystem for electric mobility. The policy will be applicable from the date of the notification," the draft stated. According to an official order, the Delhi government has invited feedback and comments on the draft policy from the public for the next 30 days. Launched in August 2020 as one of the key initiatives of the previous AAP government, the EV policy aims to tackle vehicular pollution and promote the adoption of EVs in Delhi. The policy's initial three-year term ended in August 2023 and the government has been extending it ever since. Discover the latest Business News , Sensex , and Nifty updates. Title: 'Wild West' meets 'constitutional cowboys' in Pawan Khera's explosive bail hearing- Moneycontrol.com
Description: Khera, who filed the plea on April 7, citing his Hyderabad residence, sought protection from arrest in the event of being taken into custody.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: The Telangana High Court, on Thursday, adjourned the hearing on a transit anticipatory bail plea filed by Congress leader Pawan Khera to April 10, in a case registered by Assam Police over his allegations against the wife of Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. Senior advocate Abhishek Manu Singhvi, appearing virtually for Khera, alleged that the case reflected “political vendetta” by the Assam government and argued that invoking multiple criminal provisions for alleged defamatory remarks was excessive. “We are not living in an age of constitutional cowboys… sending 100 people for what is essentially a defamation allegation is disproportionate,” Singhvi told the court, asserting that Khera was neither a habitual offender nor a flight risk. He added that the Congress leader has “deep roots in society” and would cooperate with the investigation. Khera, who filed the plea on April 7, citing his Hyderabad residence, sought protection from arrest in the event of being taken into custody. His counsel, Ponnam Ashok Goud, said orders are likely to be pronounced on Friday. Opposing the petition, Assam Advocate General Devajit Saikia rejected claims of political vendetta and argued that the plea was not maintainable before the Telangana High Court. “There is not a single indication why he cannot go to Assam. Assam is not a banana republic. Law is prevailing here,” Saikia submitted, stressing that anticipatory bail should be sought in the jurisdiction where the FIR was registered. He also alleged that Khera had misled the court and raised concerns over the maintainability of seeking relief in a different state while being a resident of Delhi. The case stems from a complaint filed by Riniki Bhuyan Sarma, who accused Khera of making false allegations during a press conference on April 5. The Congress leader had claimed that she possessed multiple passports and undisclosed foreign assets -- charges dismissed by the Sarma family as “false and malicious”. Following this, an FIR was registered at the Guwahati Crime Branch Police Station under various provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, including cheating and defamation. An Assam Police team had earlier visited Khera’s Delhi residence as part of the investigation. Defending the move, Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma said the police were acting within the law. “If there is an FIR, it is the duty of the police to act… Police are slaves of the law,” Sarma told reporters, dismissing allegations that law enforcement was being misused. After hearing arguments from both sides, the court adjourned the matter to April 10, with a decision on Khera’s anticipatory bail plea expected shortly. Title: OPINION | GAAR, Grandfathering and the Tiger Global Case: What the new rules mean for investors - Moneycontrol.com
Description: A Supreme Court ruling in January 2026 raised questions about the tax treatment of older foreign investments in India. The government responded within weeks by amending the rules
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Consider a foreign investor that invested in an Indian company in 2015. When India introduced the General Anti-Avoidance Rule (GAAR) in 2017, the government gave an explicit assurance that investments made before 1 April 2017 would not be subject to GAAR. Investors structured their affairs in reliance on that assurance. In January 2026, however, a Supreme Court ruling cast doubt on the scope of that protection for a category of investors, prompting the government to clarify the position through a rule amendment. This article traces that sequence of events and explains what the amendment does, and does not, cover. GAAR is a provision in India’s income-tax law that allows the tax authorities to look beyond the legal form of a transaction and examine whether it was structured primarily to obtain a tax benefit. If so, the tax benefit may be denied even if the transaction appears technically compliant on paper. GAAR came into effect on 1 April 2017, and it was accompanied by a specific protection: income arising from the transfer of investments made before that date would not be subject to GAAR. This protection is commonly referred to as “grandfathering” - in essence, a commitment that older investments would not be caught by the new rule. Foreign investors, particularly those investing through Mauritius, Singapore and Cyprus under India’s tax treaties with those jurisdictions, relied on this commitment while structuring their India portfolios. Tiger Global, a US-based investment fund, had invested in Flipkart through Mauritius entities between 2013 and 2016. When Walmart acquired Flipkart in 2018 in a transaction valued at approximately $16 billion, Tiger Global sold its stake and realised capital gains of around USD 1.6 billion. Tiger Global’s position was that, because the investments predated 1 April 2017, GAAR did not apply and the gains were exempt from Indian tax under the India-Mauritius tax treaty. The income-tax department took a different view. It argued that Tiger Global’s Mauritius entities lacked genuine commercial substance: there were no independent employees, no meaningful decision-making in Mauritius, and effective control rested with the fund management team in the United States. On that basis, the department contended that the structure amounted to an impermissible tax-avoidance arrangement. In its judgment dated 15 January 2026, the Supreme Court upheld the department’s position. The Court’s ruling had wider implications. It held that the date of investment alone does not determine whether GAAR applies. If an investment is held through a structure that qualifies as a tax-avoidance arrangement, GAAR may be invoked even where the investment itself predates 1 April 2017, so long as the tax benefit arises after that date. This interpretation caused concern across the investment community: if any pre-2017 investment held through an offshore structure could be examined under GAAR at the time of exit, the grandfathering assurance would have limited practical value. On 31 March 2026, the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) issued notifications amending the relevant rules under both the Income-tax Rules, 1962 and the new Income-tax Rules, 2026. The amendment clarifies that GAAR shall not be invoked in respect of income arising from the transfer of investments made before 1 April 2017, regardless of when the transfer takes place. The scope of this protection is important, especially in light of CBDT Circular No. 7 of 2017. That circular had already clarified that grandfathering extends to compulsorily convertible instruments such as compulsorily convertible debentures (CCDs) and compulsorily convertible preference shares (CCPS) acquired before 1 April 2017 and converted into equity after that date, provided the conversion terms were fixed at the time of issue. It also clarified that shares arising from bonus issues, stock splits or consolidations in respect of original holdings acquired before 1 April 2017 would be treated as part of the same grandfathered investment. The March 2026 notification reinforces these positions at the level of the rules themselves. In practical terms, if an investor acquired shares in an Indian company before 1 April 2017, income from the eventual sale of those shares should not attract GAAR, irrespective of when the sale takes place. The same position applies to shares received on conversion of pre-2017 convertible instruments and to bonus or split shares relating to pre-2017 holdings. One distinction preserved by both the circular and the notification is the difference between transfer income and holding income. While income from the sale of a pre-2017 investment is protected, dividends and interest received from the same investment after 1 April 2017 are not. The grandfathering applies to transfer income from the investment; it does not extend to recurring income generated during the holding period. An important question is whether Tiger Global - the fund whose case prompted the rule change - can itself benefit from the amendment. As the law presently stands, the answer appears to be no. The amendment is prospective and applies from 31 March 2026. Tiger Global’s tax position was already determined by the Supreme Court in January 2026, before the notification was issued. Accordingly, the legal position as it stood at the time of the judgment would continue to govern Tiger Global’s case. It may be asked whether the amendment could nevertheless be characterised as clarificatory, and therefore retrospective. Indian courts have, in limited circumstances, treated amendments as clarificatory and applied them to prior periods, including under principles discussed in Allied Motors (P) Ltd. v. CIT [1997]. However, that approach is usually available only where the earlier provision was unworkable or produced a clearly unintended result. Here, the Supreme Court upheld the original rule as legally valid and properly applicable on the facts. A provision that has been judicially affirmed in this manner is difficult to characterise as a legislative mistake. Further, the explanatory memorandum to the notification expressly states that the amendment takes effect prospectively. For investors with legacy India portfolios, the amendment restores clarity that had been missing in the period immediately following the Supreme Court’s ruling. Private equity funds, foreign portfolio investors and venture capital funds with investments made before 1 April 2017 can now evaluate exits with greater certainty on the GAAR position. The speed with which the government addressed the issue also signals an intention to preserve a stable and predictable tax environment for long-term investment. That said, the broader principles emerging from the Tiger Global ruling remain relevant. A tax residency certificate is necessary, but not sufficient, to claim treaty benefits. Foreign holding entities must still be able to demonstrate genuine commercial substance - including real decision-making, adequate personnel and actual economic presence in the jurisdiction of incorporation. The notification addresses only the GAAR grandfathering issue; it does not dilute the standards applicable to treaty eligibility. The date of investment remains central for grandfathering, but the nature and substance of the holding structure remain critical for treaty protection. (Ritesh Kumar Shyam is a Senior Tax Expert and currently serves as a Partner with Dhruva Advisors LLP.) Views expressed are personal and do not represent the stand of this publication. Title: 8th Pay Commission: How much could your take-home salary rise?- Moneycontrol.com
Description: From Rs 18,000 to Rs 58,500? The multiplier that will decide your next pay jump.
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: At the heart of the 8th Pay Commission is one number, the fitment factor. This multiplier will decide how your current basic pay translates into the new salary structure. And that, in turn, determines your actual take-home pay. Revised basic pay = Current basic pay × Fitment factor Why your take-home salary doesn’t rise in a straight line A higher fitment factor lifts your basic pay, but your take-home salary depends on more moving parts: This is why the 7th Pay Commission, despite a 2.57 fitment factor, translated into a real increase of about 14.3 percent, not a 2.57x jump. Three scenarios: what your new salary could look like If fitment factor stays at 2.57 (status quo-like scenario) Takeaway: Moderate increase, similar structure to 7th CPC transition. If fitment factor rises to 3.0 (strong revision scenario) Takeaway: Noticeable jump in take-home over time, especially after DA rebuilds. If fitment factor reaches 3.25 (aggressive hike scenario) Takeaway: Sharp increase in basic pay, but take-home boost will still phase in gradually. Your monthly salary isn’t just basic pay. It includes: Since DA is reset initially, your first payslip under the new commission may not look dramatically higher, even if your basic pay jumps. Why fitment factor is the real battleground This is why the fitment factor is not just a technical number, it’s the single biggest driver of your future salary. Even if headlines suggest a 2x or 3x jump, your actual take-home increase will be more gradual. The real gains come over time, as DA builds up on a higher base. Title: 5paisa Capital's Rs 469 cr rights issue oversubscribed 1.24 times- Moneycontrol.com
Description: The issue, which opened on March 27 and closed on Friday, received bids for approximately 1.93 crore shares against 1.56 crore shares offered, as per stock exchange data
Author: Moneycontrol
Content: Digital investment platform 5paisa Capital has closed its Rs 468.8 crore rights issue which was oversubscribed 1.24 times. The issue, which opened on March 27 and closed on Friday, received bids for approximately 1.93 crore shares against 1.56 crore shares offered, as per stock exchange data. The rights issue was priced at Rs 300 per share, with eligible shareholders entitled to subscribe to one rights share for every two equity shares held. Shares of 5paisa Capital settled 1.53 per cent higher at Rs 305.11 on the NSE. The deemed date of allotment is April 15, 2026, following which the equity shares are expected to be credited to demat accounts the same day and listed at the NSE and the BSE on April 16, 2026. MUFG Intime India is the registrar to the issue. 5paisa Capital Limited is India's first listed discount broking platform, having gone public in 2017. The company was founded by Nirmal Jain, who also established the IIFL Group. It competes with digital investment platforms such as Zerodha, Groww, Upstox and Paytm Money. The company offers a range of services including equity and derivatives trading, mutual fund investments, insurance products, and margin funding, primarily through its technology-driven platform.
